job_description:
As a C++ Engineer, you will work remotely on an hourly paid basis to review AI-generated C++ code, systems designs, and technical explanations, as well as generate high-quality reference implementations and step-by-step reasoning for complex engineering problems. You will assess solutions for accuracy, clarity, safety, and adherence to the prompt; identify errors in logic, memory handling, concurrency, or performance; fact-check technical information; write high-quality explanations and model solutions that demonstrate correct methods; and rate and compare multiple AI responses based on correctness and reasoning quality. This fully remote, hourly paid contractor role is with SME Careers, a fast-growing AI data services company and subsidiary of SuperAnnotate that provides AI training data to many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation model labs, where your C++ and systems expertise will directly help improve the world’s premier AI models used by millions of developers worldwide.|

Important

There is no immediate project for this role; however, if qualified, you will be among the first experts we reach out to when relevant opportunities arise. This will also provide you with access to future projects available through our expert network.

Key Responsibilities

• Develop AI Training Content: Create detailed prompts in various topics and responses to guide AI learning, ensuring the models reflect a comprehensive understanding of diverse subjects.
• Optimize AI Performance: Evaluate and rank AI responses to enhance the model's accuracy, fluency, and contextual relevance.
• Ensure Model Integrity: Test AI models for potential inaccuracies or biases, validating their reliability across use cases.

Your Profile

• Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Electrical/Computer Engineering, or a closely related technical field.
• 4+ years of professional experience developing production software in C++ for systems, performance-critical, or large-scale applications.
• Expert-level proficiency in modern C++ (C++11 and later), including templates, move semantics, smart pointers, and the STL.
• Strong background in systems programming concepts such as concurrency, operating systems, low-level performance optimization, and memory management.
• Hands-on experience with C++ build systems, compilers, linkers, debuggers, and profiling or analysis tools.
• Minimum C1 English proficiency (written and spoken), with the ability to write clear technical explanations and follow detailed English-language guidelines.
• Proven experience conducting detailed C++ code reviews and enforcing coding standards for safety, performance, and maintainability.
• Comfort working with version control, CI/CD workflows, and automated testing frameworks in modern C++ projects.
• Previous experience with AI data training, annotation, or evaluating AI-generated technical content is a strong plus.
• Highly detail-oriented and systematic, with a methodical approach to evaluating reasoning quality and identifying subtle issues in complex C++ code.

As an HTML / CSS Developer, you will work remotely on an hourly paid basis to review AI-generated HTML/CSS code, UI layouts, and technical explanations, as well as produce high-quality reference implementations and step-by-step reasoning for common and complex frontend tasks. You will assess solutions for correctness, clarity, accessibility, responsiveness, cross-browser compatibility, and adherence to the prompt; identify issues in semantic HTML, layout (Flexbox/Grid), responsive behavior, CSS architecture, specificity/cascade conflicts, and performance; fact-check technical information; write high-quality explanations and model solutions that demonstrate best practices; and rate and compare multiple AI responses based on correctness and reasoning quality.

This fully remote, hourly paid contractor role is with SME Careers, a fast-growing AI data services company and subsidiary of SuperAnnotate that provides AI training data to many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation model labs—where your frontend expertise will directly help improve the world’s premier AI models used by millions of developers worldwide.

Important

There is no immediate project for this role; however, if qualified, you will be among the first experts we reach out to when relevant opportunities arise. This will also provide you with access to future projects available through our expert network.

Key Responsibilities

• Develop AI Training Content: Create detailed prompts across HTML/CSS topics and write high-quality reference answers to guide AI learning.
• Optimize AI Performance: Evaluate and rank AI responses to improve semantic correctness, accessibility, responsiveness, and visual fidelity.
• Ensure Model Integrity: Test AI outputs for inaccuracies or unsafe recommendations, validating reliability across real-world UI use cases.

Your Profile

• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Design, HCI, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
• 4+ years of professional experience building production UIs with semantic HTML and modern CSS.
• Expert-level proficiency in CSS layout (Flexbox, Grid), responsive patterns, and component-based styling approaches.
• Strong accessibility knowledge: semantic structure, ARIA basics, keyboard navigation, focus management fundamentals, and WCAG-aware design.
• Strong understanding of browser behavior: box model, stacking context, positioning, rendering quirks, and cross-browser testing strategy.
• Experience with CSS maintainability: naming conventions (BEM or similar), design tokens, variables, theming, and avoiding specificity wars.
• Familiarity with modern tooling is a plus (Sass/PostCSS, Tailwind, CSS Modules, styled systems), but role focuses on core HTML/CSS correctness.
• Minimum C1 English proficiency (written and spoken), with the ability to write clear technical explanations and follow detailed English-language guidelines.
• Proven experience conducting detailed code reviews and enforcing UI quality standards for accessibility, responsiveness, and maintainability.
• Previous experience with AI data training, annotation, or evaluating AI-generated technical content is a strong plus.
• Highly detail-oriented and systematic, with a methodical approach to evaluating reasoning quality and identifying subtle issues in UI behavior.

As a Solutions Engineer, you'll be at the forefront of our customer interactions, understanding their unique business and technical needs. You'll leverage your deep technical expertise to develop and deliver compelling content, including presentations, demonstrations, workshops, and proofs-of-concept.

As a trusted technical advisor, you'll guide our customers in leveraging our cutting-edge product and adjacent technologies, including Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD, DevOps, VCS, and Cloud providers. Staying up-to-date on industry trends and emerging technologies is crucial, as you'll collaborate closely with our Product team to translate customer requirements into innovative solutions and new product features.

Your passion for continuous learning and your ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment will make you an invaluable team member. With opportunities to engage with prospects and customers at conferences, you'll have the chance to showcase your expertise and build strong relationships.

About the position
• Work with customers to understand their business and technical requirements.
• Develop and deliver technical content, including presentations, demonstrations, evaluations, workshops, and proofs-of-concept.
• Serve as a technical expert and advisor to our customers on our product and the adjacent technologies, including Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD, DevOps, VCS, and Cloud providers.
• Continuously learn and stay up to date on our product, the competition, emerging technologies, and industry trends.
• Collaborate with the Product team to translate customer business needs into technical solutions and new product requirements.
• Help with booth duty and meet with prospects and customers at conferences.

About You
• 2+ years of experience as a Solutions Engineer, a DevOps Engineer, or a similar position.
• Hands-on experience and intermediate understanding of Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, AWS CloudFormation), CI/CD, VCS, and DevOps (Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible) technologies and associated concepts, tools, and best practices.
• Hands-on experience with one or more of the main Cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP). Certifications are a plus.
• Excellent communication and presentation skills.
• Ability to work independently and in a team environment.
• Experience with software development is a plus.

Hiring Process
• 30 minutes with our Talent Acquisition Manager
• 45 minutes introductory video call with Chris Darmofal, Head of Solutions Engineering
• Take-home exercise
• Technical interview with Jubran Nassar, Solutions Engineering Tech Lead along with another Peer SE
• Final Interview with John Henry, VP of Sales
• Reference check

Why Spacelift?
• Innovative Culture: Join a team shaping cloud infrastructure management's future.
• Growth Opportunities: Be a key player in driving Spacelift's business and AWS partnership to new heights.
• Work-Life Balance: Flexible work environment, with remote-first options and competitive compensation packages.
• Collaborative Team: Work with passionate, talented people committed to driving success for our customers and partners.

What we offer

• Medical, dental and vision plans for employees and any dependents.
• 401k Pension Plan.
• 26 days of paid time off annually + local bank holidays.
• Flexible working hours and a healthy 40-hour workweek.
• Work from anywhere in the West Coast! We are a full-remote company.
• Awesome monthly allowance for benefits.

• Work in an international, diverse, dynamic, and passionate team with a friendly, supportive company culture.

job_title: Power Systems Engineer ou Ingénieur Réseau Électrique UHV).
job_description:
Company Description

Expertise Énergie & Stratégie Infrastructures is an independent organization specializing in power systems engineering and energy infrastructure strategy.

Our activities focus on high-value projects, particularly UHV networks, power generation, and electricity transmission.

The organization prioritizes consulting, engineering studies, and technical support missions for energy sector players, industrial clients, and institutional bodies.

Joining us means evolving in a flexible, autonomous environment deeply focused on technical expertise.

Job Description:

As a Power Systems Engineer / UHV Electrical Network Engineer, you will perform daily power system studies (load flow, short-circuit, stability, protection) and contribute to the design, optimization, and reliability of power transmission and generation systems.

Key Responsibilities:Analyze client requirements and define network models.

Prepare comprehensive technical study reports.

Participate in technical reviews with project teams.

Maintain a technological and regulatory watch on electrical infrastructures.

Provide strategic recommendations to support client decision-making.

This is a full-time, 100% remote position offering high autonomy in work organization, with regular virtual collaboration alongside partners and clients.

QualificationsPower Systems Expertise:

Strong command of Power Systems and Electric Power;

deep understanding of high and ultra-high voltage (UHV) transmission and distribution networks.

Network Studies:

Proven experience in Power System Studies (load flow, short-circuit, stability, power quality, protection) using specialized simulation software.

Electrical Engineering: Degree or background in Electrical Engineering with the ability to interpret electrical diagrams, technical specifications, and related standards.

Power Generation:

Solid knowledge of Power Generation (conventional and renewable power plants, grid integration, flexibility challenges, and security of supply).

Technical Tools & Skills:

Master's degree in Engineering (Engineering School or University), proficiency in network calculation software (such as PSS/E, PowerFactory, ETAP, or equivalent).

Languages & Soft Skills: Strong technical writing skills in both French and English;

sharp analytical mindset and high autonomy in a remote work environment.

Lumen is the trusted network for AI. We’re transforming how businesses connect, secure, and scale in an AI-driven world.

By connecting people, data, and applications quickly, securely, and effortlessly, we help organizations move faster and unlock what’s next.

At Lumen, people power progress. Our culture is built on teamwork, trust, and transparency, giving you the flexibility, support, and opportunity to make a lasting impact. We’re looking for top-tier talent ready to take on the challenge. Join us in building the future.
The Role

This role will lead the long-term strategic planning, optimization and governance of Adobe Real-Time CDP, including integrations with other platforms and data providers across the enterprise. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise with stakeholder-enablement experience and a passion for data-driven personalization and AI-enabled marketing.

The Main Responsibilities

Platform Ownership & Strategy
• Lead architectural strategy, implementation, and ongoing optimization of Adobe CDP within Adobe Experience Platform.
• Define and evolve data governance, identity resolution, and audience segmentation strategies in support of Growth Marketing efforts.
• Drive innovation and roadmap development for AI-ready capabilities across the platform.
Cross-Functional Collaboration
• Coordinate cross-functional support across other engineers and technical resources using AEP and AEM products.
• Foster cross-functional collaboration with Marketing, analytics, IT, and external partners.
Integration & Activation
• Oversee integration of Adobe CDP with Salesforce, Customer Journey Analytics, Adobe Journey Optimizer, and external data sources (e.g., Demandbase, ZoomInfo).
• Design and implement data ingestion pipelines using Adobe APIs, ETL tools, and native connectors.
• Ensure seamless activation of audiences across paid media, owned channels, and CRM.
Governance & Compliance
• Enforce best practices in data privacy, security, and compliance (GDPR, CCPA).
• Maintain high standards for data quality, schema design (XDM), and identity graph configuration.
Optimization & Personalization
• Support Adobe Target implementations for A/B testing, multivariate testing, and automated personalization.
• Collaborate with UX, content, and analytics teams to define hypotheses and test variants.
• Monitor performance, analyze results, and iterate based on insights.

What We Look For in a Candidate

Required Qualifications
• 5+ years of experience in Adobe Experience Platform and Adobe CDP.
• Proven track record in leading technical teams and delivering enterprise-scale personalization solutions.
• Deep understanding of Real-Time Customer Profile, Data Distiller, Segment Builder, Identity Graph, and Activation workflows.
• Strong experience with Salesforce integration and third-party data platforms (Demandbase, ZoomInfo).
• Proficiency in data modeling (SQL, JSON), XDM schema, and cloud architectures (Adobe Experience Cloud, AWS, Azure).
• Familiarity with front-end technologies (JavaScript, HTML, CSS) and tag management (Adobe Launch).
Preferred Qualifications
• Adobe certifications (e.g., Adobe Target Business Practitioner, Adobe CDP Architect).
• Experience with Adobe Target.
• Experience with AI/ML models in marketing use cases.
• Knowledge of marketing automation platforms and campaign orchestration tools.
• Experience integrating Adobe stack with other enterprise platforms (Salesforce, DemandBase, LeanData).
• Working knowledge of API endpoints supporting the individual tools, including both ingestion and feed mechanisms. This is critical for enabling AI-driven capabilities and personalization strategies.
• Familiarity with Agile/Scaled Agile methodologies.

Compensation

job_title: Hiring Event | Virtual & In-Person | Cyber Embedded Engineer | Dallas
job_description:
Job Description

Applying to this Expression of Interest opportunity introduces you to Lockheed Martin's Cyber opportunities and promotes you to managers who are interested in hiring all levels of Experienced Professionals. Selected candidates will have the opportunity to meet hiring leaders, learn more about our organization, and discuss exciting career opportunities available at our Dallas site. Qualified applicants may be invited to interview during the event, providing a streamlined path to connect directly with decision-makers and explore current openings.

If you are interested in building your career in Dallas TX, we encourage you to apply early, as interview invitations will be extended on a rolling basis.

Apply today and take the first step toward your next career opportunity!

You will be a Cyber Security EMBEDDED Engineer in the Program Protection Organization. Our organization oversees all aspects of Anti-Tamper and embedded Cybersecurity across Missiles and Fire Control. We are chartered with providing the skills and innovative technologies to develop, design, produce and sustain secure products across our Lines of Business.

What You Will Be Doing
As the Cyber Security Engineer you will be responsible for providing embedded System Security Engineering design and implementation in all aspects of System Security Engineering (SSE) process in development of Department of Defense (DoD) Weapon Systems. Your responsibilities will include:
• Assessing and mitigating System Security threats/risks throughout the embedded Weapon System development life cycle.
• Validating System Security Engineering requirements decomposition, allocation and analysis for embedded Weapon Systems.
• Implementing System Security Engineering solutions hardware and software.
• Verifying implementation of system security requirements.
• Designing, implementing, deploying, and maintaining state of the art System Security architectures and countermeasures to protect and enable innovative new Lockheed Martin Missiles and Fire Control products.
• Analyzing weaknesses, vulnerabilities and threats against System Security posture on product designs.
• Prototyping and testing System Security countermeasures that defend against cyber-attacks against Lockheed Martin Missiles and Fire Control Weapon System products.
• For a level 3, we typically look for a Bachelors degree from an accredited college.

Why Join Us

We are looking for a collaborative, purpose-driven professional who thrives on solving complex engineering challenges that keep the warfighter safe. This role offers high visibility, direct impact on mission-critical programs, and a clear path for career growth within a world-class aerospace organization.

We are committed to supporting your work-life balance and overall well-being, offering flexible scheduling options. Learn more about Lockheed Martin's comprehensive benefits package here.

Further Information About This Opportunity

This position is in Dallas. Discover more about our Dallas, Texas location.

MUST BE A U.S. CITIZEN - This position is located at a facility that requires special access. The selected candidate must have the ability to obtain Secret clearance.

Basic Qualifications
• Experience working within the DoD systems development life-cycle (SDLC)
• Experience with embedded systems design, microcontroller programming, and real-time operating systems (RTOS).
• Implementing System Security requirements on DoD systems to meet U.S. government compliance requirements.
• Experience supporting validation and verification of security requirements on DoD systems.
• Possess an approved DoD 8570 baseline certification for IAT/IAM Level II (CISSP, CAP, etc.) or ability to obtain certification within 6 months of start date in order to meet program requirements.

Desired skills

- Experience in cybersecurity / Anti Tamper principles
- Aircraft Interface
- Serial Interface
- Ethernet Interface
- IMU
- GPS
- IR Sensors
- Data Links
-Programming experience with C, C++, python, Java, MATLAB

Work Schedule Information

Lockheed Martin supports a variety of alternate work schedules that provide additional flexibility to our employees. Schedules range from standard 40 hours over a five day work week while others may be condensed. These condensed schedules provide employees with additional time away from the office and are in addition to our Paid Time off benefits.

National Pay Statement

Pay Rate: The annual base salary range for this position in California, Massachusetts, and New York (excluding most major metropolitan areas), Colorado, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Minnesota, New Jersey, Vermont, Washington or Washington DC is $101,000 - $178,135. For states not referenced above, the salary range for this position will reflect the candidate's final work location. Please note that the salary information is a general guideline only. Lockheed Martin considers factors such as (but not limited to) scope and responsibilities of the position, candidate's work experience, education/ training, key skills as well as market and business considerations when extending an offer.
Benefits offered: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, Short-Term Disability, Long-Term Disability, 401(k) match, Flexible Spending Accounts, EAP, Education Assistance, Parental Leave, Paid time off, and Holidays.
(Washington state applicants only) Non-represented full-time employees: accrue at least 10 hours per month of Paid Time Off (PTO) to be used for incidental absences and other reasons; receive at least 90 hours for holidays. Represented full time employees accrue 6.67 hours of Vacation per month; accrue up to 52 hours of sick leave annually; receive at least 96 hours for holidays. PTO, Vacation, sick leave, and holiday hours are prorated based on start date during the calendar year.
This position is incentive plan eligible.

Premium Pay Statement

Pay Rate: The annual base salary range for this position in most major metropolitan areas in California, Massachusetts, and New York is $116,200 - $201,365. For states not referenced above, the salary range for this position will reflect the candidate's final work location. Please note that the salary information is a general guideline only. Lockheed Martin considers factors such as (but not limited to) scope and responsibilities of the position, candidate's work experience, education/ training, key skills as well as market and business considerations when extending an offer.
Benefits offered: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, Short-Term Disability, Long-Term Disability, 401(k) match, Flexible Spending Accounts, EAP, Education Assistance, Parental Leave, Paid time off, and Holidays.
This position is incentive plan eligible.

Key Responsibilities
• Supervise, train and mentor the team of graduates and interns
• Develop and support the execution of mine designs and plans for the Mining operations
• Lead by example – Seek opportunities to systematically support efforts in leading and improving safety in all areas of the operation
• Monitor, track and report operational execution against mine plans. Highlight deviations to the plan and identify opportunities to catch up or exceed the plan
• Manage and oversee all contractors’ activities related to the Mine Engineering
• Design and optimize open pit and mine layouts, including haul roads, ramps, stopes, and sequencing
• Ensure all mine plans comply with safety standards, regulatory requirements, and environmental guidelines
• Collaborate closely with exploration, operations/mining contractors, permitting, and other site teams to align plans and ensure successful execution
• Support and manage engineering consultants on studies as required
• Monitor mine performance and reconcile actual results against plan; identify variances and implement improvements
• Conduct economic evaluations, cost analysis, and productivity assessments
• Mentor and supervise junior mine engineers and technical staff
• Support budgeting, forecasting, and life-of-mine planning activities and capital projects
• Implement and maintain mine planning software and systems
• Develop mine planning processes and procedures
• Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives and operational excellence programs
• Perform other duties and projects as assigned

Required Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in Mining Engineering or a related discipline required; Master’s degree preferred.
• Minimum of 8 years of open pit mining experience with expertise in mine planning, optimization, and operations.
• Proficiency with mine planning software such as Deswik and Vulcan, with a strong understanding of drill and blast, geotechnical engineering, and mine economics.
• Working knowledge of U.S. mining regulations, including MSHA standards, and a demonstrated commitment to safe mining practices.
• Strong analytical, problem-solving, and strategic planning skills with the ability to evaluate both short- and long-term impacts of mine planning decisions.
• Proven leadership, communication, and cross-functional collaboration skills, with the ability to build credibility, mentor teams, and support organizational priorities.
• Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, remote site environment while maintaining a high level of attention to detail, operational awareness, and efficiency.

Work Environment

This is a full-time, permanent position based on-site at Round Top Mountain, Sierra Blanca, TX, in an outdoor desert environment with exposure to heat, dust, and remote conditions. The role grows with the project through construction and into full mining operations, with occasional travel required.

USA Rare Earth proudly supports Equal Employment Opportunities

USA Rare Earth's policy is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and will provide reasonable accommodations according to applicable federal, state, and/or local laws.

For assistance applying for employment through this site due to disability, please contact Human Resources, hr@usare.com, to discuss reasonable accommodations.

Company Background

Washington Equipment Manufacturing Company, Inc. (WEMCO, Inc.) is a contract manufacturer specializing in the design, engineering, and manufacturing of productivity-enhancing solutions, including overhead cranes for material handling, automated aerospace tooling, agricultural equipment, and custom machinery used across multiple industries worldwide.

Unlike a traditional consulting firm, WEMCO is a fully integrated manufacturer with in-house capabilities including engineering, machining, fabrication, welding, painting, assembly, testing, installation, and field service. As part of our engineering team, you'll see your designs become reality while collaborating directly with the people who build and install them.

Visit www.wemcoinc.com to learn more about our work.

Location

Remote (Nashville, TN)

Applicants should reside in or near the Nashville metropolitan area to support occasional customer visits, project meetings, and travel as needed.

Job Purpose

WEMCO is seeking an experienced Professional Electrical Engineer (PE) to join our growing Engineering team in a remote capacity based in the Nashville, TN area. This position will support engineering projects across multiple industries by designing, reviewing, and improving industrial electrical and electromechanical systems for overhead cranes, custom machinery, and automated equipment.

Working closely with mechanical, structural, and controls engineers, you will be responsible for developing practical, manufacturable solutions while collaborating with our manufacturing facilities and field service personnel throughout project execution.

Occasional travel to customer sites and WEMCO facilities will be required for project coordination, commissioning support, and engineering reviews.

Job Responsibilities

• Design and evaluate industrial electrical and electromechanical systems utilizing principles of electrical engineering, mechanics, hydraulics, pneumatics, and power systems.

• Design, review, and improve industrial control systems based on customer requirements and project specifications.

• Collaborate with multidisciplinary engineering teams to identify and resolve design, manufacturing, and performance issues.

• Develop electrical drawings, schematics, panel layouts, and documentation using AutoCAD and related engineering software.

• Create testing procedures and provide remote and on-site technical support during equipment assembly, startup, and commissioning.

• Execute engineering assignments while maintaining project schedules and budgets.

• Perform root cause analysis and develop corrective actions for equipment failures and field issues.

• Prepare technical reports, calculations, engineering documentation, and customer presentations.

• Coordinate with manufacturing personnel and field technicians to resolve installation or wiring questions during fabrication and field installation.

• Assist with procurement and specification of electrical components for control panels and machinery.

• Develop operation and maintenance manuals for engineered systems.

• Provide technical support to internal departments and customers.

• Ensure designs comply with applicable codes, customer specifications, and industry standards.

• Support project planning, scheduling, and continuous improvement initiatives.

• Maintain strong working relationships with customers, vendors, field personnel, and internal departments.

• Design for manufacturability using accepted engineering and drafting standards.

• Troubleshoot electrical systems and develop practical engineering solutions to project challenges.

• Travel periodically to customer facilities, project sites, and WEMCO locations as required.

• Maintain knowledge of applicable standards including ISO 9001, AS9100, OSHA, ANSI, ASME, CMAA, AWS, NEC, NFPA 70E, and other applicable engineering codes and standards.

Qualifications

Required

• Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering

• Professional Engineer (PE) License in Electrical Engineering

• 3–5 years of experience in industrial automation, controls, or heavy industrial equipment

• Strong understanding of industrial electrical systems, motors, VFDs, PLCs, hydraulic systems, and pneumatic systems

• Experience with AutoCAD (Autodesk Inventor experience preferred)

• Strong programming and troubleshooting skills related to industrial controls

• Ability to work independently remotely, while effectively collaborating with multidisciplinary teams

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills

• Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities

• Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously

• Valid driver's license

• Ability to obtain and maintain a U.S. Passport with no travel restrictions

Preferred

• Experience designing overhead cranes, lifting equipment, or heavy industrial machinery

• Familiarity with UL 508A control panels

• Experience with PLC and HMI programming

• Working knowledge of NEC, NFPA 70E, and industrial electrical safety standards

• Experience supporting field commissioning and startup activities

Benefits

• Competitive Salary

• 401(k) Retirement Plan

• Medical Insurance

• Dental Insurance

• Vision Insurance

• Paid Time Off

• Paid Holidays

• Employee Referral Program

• Remote Work Flexibility

• Supplemental Insurance through Colonial Life

• Opportunities for Professional Development and Continuing Education

• Occasional Travel to Customer Sites and WEMCO Facilities

Join Our Team

If you're looking for an opportunity where your engineering work directly impacts the products being built—and where you'll collaborate with a talented team dedicated to innovation, quality, and customer success—we encourage you to apply.

At WEMCO, your designs don't sit on a shelf. They get built.

Construction Engineering practice works with fabricators, erectors, construction managers and general contractors to provide connection design, temporary stability analysis, design of structures delegated to the contractor, design of industrial structures, and construction means-and-methods engineering. There will be an emphasis on the development of relationships with current, but also new clients to grow the practices revenue potential. The core areas of focus for growth are providing structural and construction engineering services for the construction industry.

The Role

We have an exciting opportunity for a Structural Associate to join our team in Phoenix, Arizona. This position offers the unique opportunity to support a major, high-profile construction project on-site while also becoming part of Thornton Tomasetti's new Phoenix office and helping establish our presence in one of the country's fastest-growing markets.

Responsibilities
• Manage the direction, coordination, planning, organization, control, integration, and completion of engineering projects.
• Communicate with clients to understand and deliver project requirements.
• Develop new clients and revenue channels.
• Plan and formulate engineering program and organize project staff according to project requirements.
• Analysis and design of Industrial and Advanced Manufacturing buildings and structures.
• Manage project design and deliverables as well as other design team members, supervise the work of engineers and modelers, and direct project staff in preparing contract documents such as drawings and specifications.
• Direct project staff in preparing contract documents.
• Responsible for team, project, and deliverable QAQC

Requirements
• 8-12 years of experience.
• Degree (master preferred) in structural engineering or related field.
• Strong technical skills in the areas of structural design of structural steel and concrete structures.
• Strong working knowledge in the area of steel connection design.
• Working knowledge of current industry software, including Microsoft Excel, Mathcad, Tekla Structures, Revit, and AutoCAD.
• Experience in managing a team of 3-8 people.
• Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
• PE and/or SE license.
• Ability and willingness to travel within the US and internationally.

Benefits

Depending on your employment status, benefits may include:
• Medical, Dental, Vision, Life, AD&D, Disability and other voluntary benefits
• Flexible Spending Accounts for Medical & Childcare
• Paid Time Off, Family Leave for New Parents, Volunteer Time
• Tuition Reimbursement
• Commuter Transit (where available)
• 401k retirement savings with Company matching on employee contributions and/or qualified student loan repayments
• Fitness Reimbursement
• And other various wellness, diversity/inclusion and employee resource programs and initiatives

job_title: Application Engineer - Wireline and Energy Services
job_description:
Job Summary:

The Application Engineer – Wireline & Energy Services is a senior technical role supporting wireline and oilfield service applications across testing, data management, and cross-functional execution.

Reporting to the Manager, Engineering, this position is responsible for overseeing all wireline applications testing, maintaining and improving technical data management practices, acting as the technical bridge between engineering, operations, R&D, and commercial teams, and implementing standards that ensure data integrity and service quality.

Working with the team, the position also contributes to the development and implementation of new technology solutions

Key Accountabilities:

Applications Testing (Wireline and Field-Focused)

- Providing technical client facing support for the development/design/validation of any tests
- The development of test programs
- Implementation of process around testing to ensure safe and efficient performance of all tests
- Design and capture all data requirements around testing
- Report generation
Data Management and Integrity

- Develop, implement, and improve systems, processes, and policies to manage job, results, and service quality data
- Establish/maintain data standards, workflows, procedures, and inventory of operational data
- Ensure functionality, integrity, and accessibility of data management systems and solutions
- Coordinate with internal and external specialists to analyze and interpret technical data
Cross-Functional Collaboration and Technical Support

- Collaborate with all departments to share best practices and ensure alignment
- Support Sales and Marketing through validation/creation of case studies, job information forms, job programs, and technical papers
- Participate in business reviews on all applications, engineering and research proposals
- Provide technical oversight on sales tools and documents (e.g., competitor comparisons, revenue calculators, tool data sheets)
- Coordinate technical input for project execution with subject matter experts
- Validate production and equipment treatment data in Salesforce
- Participate and provide input in all R&D/Engineering planning efforts
Service Quality, Innovation, and Industry Engagement

- Lead service quality initiatives, including root cause analysis and failure resolution
- Participate in R&D direction through data analysis, SRED tracking, and tool/testing planning
- Create treatment programs, final treatment reports, and screen wells for candidate potential
- Contribute to technical communication through case studies, white papers, and SPE papers
- Proofread and edit technical documents and represent the company in alignment with its core values
Required Qualifications & Competencies (including Licenses/Certifications):

- Minimum 5–7+ years of wireline industry experience
- A relevant degree or diploma from a recognized post-secondary institute, preferably in science or engineering, or equivalent industry experience
- 7+ years’ experience in engineering, applications engineering, or product development within oil and gas or energy services
- Advanced technical proficiency across multiple technology platforms (Office, Data Analytics, Business Intelligence tools) with proven expertise in report creation, modeling and trend forecasting
- Able to analyze and interpret geoscience, petrophysical, and production data and to present results, recommendations and/or best practices
- Exceptional attention to detail with a demonstrated interest in research and data analysis
- Highly collaborative workstyle combined with a capacity for problem solving and achieving results
- Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to present technical information clearly and influence across technical and non-technical teams
Behavioral Competencies:

- Systems Mindset
- Strategic Thinking
- Decision Making
- Teamwork
- Communication
- Data Analysis
Living our Core Values: Inspiring Energetic Change:

- WE/ME: We act and make decisions for the benefit of the team, not for our own ego or advancement. Like family we are committed to the success and well-being of every Sparky.
- Dependable:We say what we do and then we do what we say even when no one is watching
- Solve it!:When we see problem, we fix it using ingenuity and common sense. We have the courage to fail fast and learn.
- Fun Works:We take ourselves lightly and our work seriously. Optimism, kindness and a sense of humor go a long way when the going gets tough. We can laugh at ourselves and with each other when sharing setbacks or celebrating success.
By submitting your application, you consent to the collection, use, and disclosure of your personal information for the purposes of recruitment, assessment, and selection in accordance with applicable privacy legislation. We thank all candidates for their interest; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

job_title: Freelance Tabletop Product Designer (Seasonal Ceramics & Gift)
job_description:
Company Description

Home Essentials & Beyond is a leading wholesaler specializing in home décor, tabletop, and seasonal products for major off-price retailers including TJMaxx, HomeGoods, Marshalls, Burlington, Ross, and other national accounts.

We are a design-driven organization focused on creating trend-right, commercially successful products across seasonal and everyday categories. We are looking for talented freelance designers to support our growing tabletop and gift business.

Role Description

We are seeking a Freelance Seasonal Tabletop Designer to join our team on a project basis.

The ideal candidate will have experience developing commercial tabletop and gift collections, with a strong focus on seasonal ceramics, mugs, dinnerware, serveware, and home décor collections.

Responsibilities include:

* Developing seasonal ceramic collections for Spring, Halloween, Christmas, and Everyday categories.
* Designing coordinated collections including mugs, dinnerware, cookie jars, canisters, serveware, and gift items.
* Researching market trends and translating trends into commercially viable retail products.

Qualifications

* Proven experience designing tabletop, ceramics, giftware, or home décor collections.
* Strong understanding of commercial retail and off-price business models.
* Experience designing products for retailers such as TJMaxx, HomeGoods, Marshalls, Burlington, Ross, Mud Pie , Pottery Barn, Anthropologie, or similar retailers preferred.
* Ability to create commercially viable collections that balance trend, price point, and manufacturability.
* Strong Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop skills.
* Experience working with overseas factories and preparing production-ready artwork is a plus.
* Excellent communication skills and ability to work independently.
* Proficiency in Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop required. Ability to provide editable Adobe Illustrator (.AI) vector files and production-ready artwork is required.

Portfolio submission is required. Applicants must provide examples of seasonal tabletop, ceramic, mug, dinnerware, giftware, or home décor collections. Applicants without relevant portfolio examples will not be considered.

This is a remote freelance position on a project-by-project basis. Candidates must be able to work independently, manage deadlines, and communicate effectively in a remote environment.

Company Website:
https://homeessentials.com

BAGGU is a design focused bag company based in San Francisco, California. We are a small but hard-working team of people dedicated to making cool bags that feel good to use.

Please note that info@baggu.com is used for Customer Experience inquiries - please do not use this channel for Application inquiries.

WHY DEPT®?

We are a Growth Invention company built to help the world’s most ambitious brands grow faster. Operating at the intersection of technology and marketing, we create what is next by pioneering ideas, acting fast, and moving further because standing still just is not in our DNA.

We are drawn to people who stay curious, move with intent, and never stop inventing. Our culture runs on three values: better together, relentlessly curious, and get sh*t done. It is how we work, how we grow, and how we make things that matter.

At DEPT®, you will find the freedom to explore, the space to collaborate, and the trust to make a real impact for our clients, for each other, and for the world we are helping to build.

THE ROLE:

We are looking for a talented Senior, Motion Designer to help bring creative ideas to life through motion, animation, video, and emerging digital experiences. This is a hands-on role for a highly skilled motion designer who combines strong storytelling instincts, technical expertise, and a passion for creating engaging content across social, digital, product, and brand experiences.

Working closely with Creative Directors, Art Directors, Copywriters, Visual Designers, Generative AI Artists, and cross-functional partners, you will transform creative concepts into compelling motion experiences that connect with audiences and elevate the overall quality of the work.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in collaborative environments, enjoys solving creative challenges through motion, and has a strong point of view on how movement, pacing, sound, and storytelling work together to create impactful experiences. You are equally comfortable developing ideas, executing them at a high level, and helping raise the bar for motion craft across the team.

WHAT YOU’LL DO:

• Conceptualize, design, and execute high-quality motion graphics, animation, UI motion, video content, and digital experiences across campaigns and content initiatives.
• Develop storyboards, style frames, motion studies, and visual explorations that help communicate and refine creative concepts.
• Partner closely with Creative Directors, Art Directors, Copywriters, Visual Designers, and Generative AI Artists to create integrated audience experiences across channels and platforms.
• Collaborate on music selection, sound design, and audio direction to enhance storytelling, reinforce brand expression, and elevate the overall quality of motion experiences.
• Ensure all motion work maintains a high standard of craft, storytelling, technical execution, and brand consistency.
• Contribute to pre-production planning, production, and post-production efforts to help ensure creative intent is maintained through final delivery.
• Explore AI-assisted motion workflows, automation, and emerging creative technologies that improve efficiency and unlock new creative possibilities.
• Manage multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining exceptional attention to detail, organization, and quality.
• Participate in creative reviews, brainstorms, and cultural trend exploration, bringing fresh inspiration and motion-first thinking to the team.
• Stay current on motion design, animation, filmmaking, sound design, AI, and emerging technologies to continually push the work forward.

WHAT YOU BRING:

• 2+ years of professional experience as a Motion Designer, Animator, or similar role.
• A strong, diverse portfolio showcasing outstanding motion design, animation skills, and a solid understanding of design principles (typography, color, layout).
• Expert knowledge of Figma and Adobe After Effects, Adobe Premiere a plus.
• Deep understanding of AI workflows and tools.
• Bachelor's degree in Graphic Design, Animation, Film, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.

WHAT DO WE OFFER?

We are a collaborative, open-door, best-idea-wins environment that fosters personal and company growth and has fun doing it. Here is a snapshot of our benefits package:

• Healthcare, Dental, and Vision coverage
• 401k plan, plus matching
• PTO
• Paid Company Holidays
• Parental Leave

IT Systems Implementation Engineer

Data Integration, Analytics & Agentic AI Solutions

Full Time

Remote - US

Reports to: Director of Product Implementation and Enablement

Innovate with Benchmark Gensuite as an IT Systems Implementation Engineer

Everyone wants to make the world a better place. We work to make it happen. By joining Benchmark Gensuite and exploring careers in the EHS, Sustainability, and ESG Disclosure Reporting space, you’ll be helping customers throughout the business community empower informed decision-making, improve employee safety, and lessen their environmental footprint while creating a culture that values diversity, equity, and inclusion. Join us and help make the corporate world a better place for the planet and better for its employees.

Benchmark Gensuite jobs play a crucial role in helping develop our unified EHS, Sustainability, and ESG digital transformation software solutions that enable cross-functional performance excellence, enhance businesses’ sustainability efforts, and help them achieve their ESG goals. With our incredible global team, the collaborative spirit of our subscribers and partners, and the continuous innovation in exciting new technologies like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and IoT, a career at Benchmark Gensuite means you will continuously use and evolve your skills to develop fresh ideas and initiatives.

Benchmark Gensuite Benefits

At Benchmark Gensuite, your hard work is rewarded. We offer annual bonus programs, regular team-building events, and off-cycle reviews to promptly recognize and reward excellent performers. With engaging team activities including happy hours and exciting virtual events, we actively encourage our teams to connect and build a strong, supportive, and fun rapport! Our Benchmark Gensuite Team Member Networks, like the Respect for Diversity Group and the Sustainability Group, offer a safe and supportive space, bringing together team members who share a common identity and empowering them to voice their ideas and initiate action. Explore Benchmark Gensuite job openings and come join us as we combine our collective creativity and passion to meet and exceed the expectations of our customers.

About Benchmark Gensuite…

Benchmark Gensuite® enables companies to implement robust, cross-functional digital systems for EHS, Sustainability, and ESG Reporting through a unified digital platform—locally, globally, and across diverse operating profiles.

With intuitive, best-practice-based process functionality, flexible configurations, and powerful extensions, the Benchmark Gensuite® platform has helped companies worldwide manage their EHS, Sustainability; Quality; Operational Risk and Compliance; Product Stewardship, and Supply Chain Risks for over two decades; and now organically integrated with cutting-edge ESG disclosure reporting and management solutions. Join over 3 million users that trust Benchmark Gensuite® with their software system needs and benefit from rapid deployment and adoption, immediate return on investment (ROI), service excellence, and collaborative innovation.

About The Role

Benchmark Gensuite is seeking a hands-on IT Systems Implementations Engineer to serve as the technical delivery owner for Subscriber-facing integration and AI implementation projects. This is a customer-facing, high-impact role for a professional who combines strong engineering instincts with the communication skills to work confidently alongside enterprise IT teams.

You will lead end-to-end delivery of systems integration projects—from technical discovery through go-live—working directly with Subscriber IT organizations to define requirements, design solutions, and drive implementation. A key emerging focus of this role is the deployment of Agentic AI solutions within Subscriber environments, enabling intelligent workflow automation on top of our SaaS platform. This role carries a clear growth path toward a Forward Deployed Engineer position for the right candidate.

Key Responsibilities

Subscriber Engagement & Technical Discovery

• Serve as the primary technical implementation contact for Subscriber IT teams across integration, analytics, and AI deployment engagements.
• Lead technical discovery sessions to elicit, document, and validate integration and automation requirements.
• Build trusted working relationships with Subscriber stakeholders ranging from IT managers and system administrators to enterprise architects.
• Translate complex, ambiguous customer requirements into clear and actionable engineering specifications.

Systems Integration & Implementation

• Own end-to-end delivery of systems integration projects including HR data synchronization, Single Sign-On (SSO/SAML/SCIM) implementations, application-specific data migrations, and API-driven record updates.
• Design and document data mapping specifications, API integration flows, and system architecture diagrams.
• Configure, test, and validate integrations in collaboration with Subscriber IT teams and internal Engineering.
• Troubleshoot integration failures, data discrepancies, and connectivity issues across implementation phases.
• Manage project scope, timelines, risks, and stakeholder communications across concurrent Subscriber engagements.

Agentic AI Solution Deployment

• Partner with internal Engineering and Product teams to implement Agentic AI capabilities within subscriber instances.
• Engage Subscriber IT teams to assess workflow automation opportunities and define requirements for AI agent implementations.
• Support configuration, testing, and rollout of AI-driven automation solutions including intelligent data processing, decision support agents, and workflow orchestration.
• Stay current on emerging Agentic AI platforms, frameworks, and SaaS integration patterns relevant to the enterprise EHS and compliance domain.
• Serve as a knowledgeable resource to Subscribers navigating their AI adoption journey, helping them understand capabilities, limitations, and best practices.

Documentation & Continuous Improvement

• Maintain comprehensive project documentation including requirements specs, integration design documents, test plans, and go-live runbooks.
• Contribute to internal knowledge bases, reusable integration templates, and implementation playbooks.
• Provide structured feedback to Product and Engineering teams based on Subscriber implementation experiences.

Required Qualifications

• 3–5 years of experience in a systems implementation, solutions engineering, IT integration, or technical consulting role.
• Solid working knowledge of systems integration concepts, RESTful APIs, webhooks, and data workflow design.
• Hands-on experience with one or more of the following: SSO/SAML/SCIM protocols, HRIS platform integrations (e.g., Workday, ADP, SAP SuccessFactors), ETL pipelines, or enterprise data migration projects.
• Experience deploying or configuring AI-driven automation within enterprise SaaS platforms.
• Demonstrated ability to lead technical conversations and communicate complex concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
• Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent customer-facing implementation projects with competing timelines.
• Strong documentation skills—comfortable producing requirements specs, data maps, API specs, and integration design artifacts.
• Proficiency with project tracking and collaboration tools such as Jira, Confluence, Asana, or equivalent.

Preferred Qualifications

• Exposure to Agentic AI platforms, LLM-based workflow automation, or AI agent frameworks (e.g., LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, or vendor-native AI agents in enterprise SaaS).
• Familiarity with AI orchestration concepts such as tool use, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), prompt chaining, and multi-agent coordination.
• Familiarity with iPaaS platforms such as MuleSoft, Workato, Dell Boomi, or similar.
• Working knowledge of API testing tools such as Postman or Swagger.
• Basic scripting or query skills (SQL, Python, or similar) for data validation and troubleshooting.
• Exposure to EHS, compliance, or workforce management software platforms.
• PMP, CAPM, or equivalent project management certification.

Career Growth

This role is designed to grow with you. High-performing candidates will have the opportunity to advance into a Forward Deployed Engineer role—taking on deeper technical ownership of integration architecture, Agentic AI implementations, custom development, and strategic Subscriber partnerships.

Questions about the position? Please contact our HR Team, at jobs@benchmarkdigital.com.

Your Job

DEPCOM Power, a Koch Engineered Solutions company, is looking for an Engineer - Services. This role sits in DEPCOM's Field Services capability supporting the Services department and can be based in Scottsdale, AZ, Bedminster, NJ, or fully remote. The primary function of this engineering role is to support the repowering, restoration, augmentation, operation, and maintenance of utility scale PV and BESS plants. This role will require 25-40% travel to accomplish business objectives. 

This role does not provide VISA sponsorship. 

Our Team

At Koch Industries, our Principle Based Management philosophy is at the center of how we do business, and as an employee, you will begin your journey of understanding and applying the way we do business in your everyday work. 

What You Will Do

• As a self-starter, define and improve processes from project origination through execution.
• Generate tailored solutions, prepare clear Scopes of Work, and perform detailed site assessments to align with customer needs, reduce risks, and achieve measurable sales and business development success.
• Create construction drawings, design reports, calculations, product specifications, and procurement support documents, along with bills of materials and Scopes of Work, to ensure compliance, optimize procurement, and enable efficient contract execution for successful project outcomes.
• Collaborate with the construction team to promptly address RFIs from owners, subcontractors, or field personnel, ensuring seamless communication, minimizing delays, and maintaining alignment with project objectives for efficient execution.
• Performs detailed cost/benefit analyses for cable constructions, grounding schemes, and major equipment to optimize project costs, enhance system reliability, and support safe, compliant, and effective project execution.
• Deliver high-quality engineering solutions by adhering to DEPCOM’s standard procedures, collaborating with SMEs and equipment OEMs to complete tasks, overseeing first article inspections and quality assessments, and mentoring team members to foster growth and operational excellence.
• Understanding of energy markets and how various customer types (utility, IPP, developer, etc.) view/prioritize services and equipment selection.
• Demonstrated ability to blend financial thinking and technical/operational risk mitigation to create solutions that lead to mutual benefit.

Who You Are (Basic Qualifications)

• Bachelor’s Degree or higher in Engineering
• Experience with photovoltaic (PV) design and engineering in the power and utility sector
• Exposure to after-market and legacy equipment as well as O&M offerings through OEM, EPC, or subcontracting.
• Experience and understanding of electrical design calculations, including load flow, short circuit, protective device coordination, arc flash (AC & DC), ground grid analysis, and ampacity calculations
• Knowledge of National Electrical Code, National Electrical Safety Code and NFPA 70E

What Will Put You Ahead (Preferred Qualifications)

• Bachelor’s Degree or higher in Electrical Engineering
• Experience working in the field of power plant construction
• Experience working for an EPC (Engineering, Procurement, Construction) company
• Experience in energy storage applications and/or integration into utility scale solar
• Professional Engineer P.E. License

For this role, we anticipate paying $110,000 - $140,000 per year. This role is eligible for variable pay, issued as a monetary bonus or in another form.

At Koch companies, we are entrepreneurs. This means we openly challenge the status quo, find new ways to create value and get rewarded for our individual contributions. Any compensation range provided for a role is an estimate determined by available market data. The actual amount may be higher or lower than the range provided considering each candidate's knowledge, skills, abilities, and geographic location. If you have questions, please speak to your recruiter about the flexibility and detail of our compensation philosophy.

Hiring Philosophy

All Koch companies value diversity of thought, perspectives, aptitudes, experiences, and backgrounds. We are Military Ready and Second Chance employers. Learn more about our hiring philosophy here.

Who We Are

At Koch, employees are empowered to do what they do best to make life better. Learn how our business philosophy helps employees unleash their potential while creating value for themselves and the company. As a Koch Engineered Solutions company, DEPCOM Power is a leading energy solutions partner for the utility solar and broader energy industries, providing project development support, engineering, procurement and construction, energy storage, repowering and operations and maintenance services. Our highly skilled team of conventional and renewable power industry experts work to deliver the lowest cost of energy and the highest rate of return

Our Benefits

Our goal is for each employee, and their families, to live fulfilling and healthy lives. We provide essential resources and support to build and maintain physical, financial, and emotional strength - focusing on overall wellbeing so you can focus on what matters most. Our benefits plan includes - medical, dental, vision, flexible spending and health savings accounts, life insurance, ADD, disability, retirement, paid vacation/time off, educational assistance, and may also include infertility assistance, paid parental leave and adoption assistance. Specific eligibility criteria is set by the applicable Summary Plan Description, policy or guideline and benefits may vary by geographic region. If you have questions on what benefits apply to you, please speak to your recruiter.

Additionally, everyone has individual work and personal needs. We seek to enable the best work environment that helps you and the business work together to produce superior results.

Equal Opportunities

Equal Opportunity Employer, including disability and protected veteran status. Except where prohibited by state law, some offers of employment are conditioned upon successfully passing a drug test. job_title: Managed Services Engineer I (Pacific Time hours)
job_description:
Managing modern technology has become increasingly complex. As organizations scale across clouds, endpoints, and hybrid environments, IT operations and cybersecurity often remain fragmented, creating gaps in visibility, slowing response times, and increasing risk.
Logically closes that gap.

We unify IT operations and cybersecurity into a single, accountable operating model that reduces risk, accelerates response, and strengthens resilience across the systems businesses depend on. With shared visibility, integrated workflows, and clear ownership, organizations gain greater control, fewer blind spots, and more confidence in their technology.

Our approach is cyber-first by design, embedding protection into every layer, from end user to edge to cloud. By combining AI-assisted monitoring with human-led expertise, we deliver faster insight, smarter decisions, and more effective outcomes without the complexity of siloed tools.

For more than 20 years, Logically has partnered with mid-market and enterprise organizations to simplify complexity, reduce risk, and improve resilience, acting as a true extension of their teams with clear accountability and measurable outcomes.
Logically. Cyber-First. Future-Ready.
Why Work for Us
• A culture built on doing the right thing
• Work at the forefront of cybersecurity and emerging technologies
• Collaborative, growth-oriented environment
• Competitive compensation and benefits
• Opportunities for professional development and advancement
• Make a real impact by helping organizations reduce risk and build resilience
Job Summary:

The Managed Services Engineer I works as a member of a collaborative national support team that provides technical support to the clients of Logically. The Managed Services Engineer I will be expected to work on a variety of infrastructure environments which may include but are not limited to Windows Servers (Physical/Virtual), Windows Workstations, Switches, Firewalls, Access points, Data Storage Systems. In addition to this you will work with both Logically clients and vendors to resolve customer issues and requests in a timely manner.Pacific Time work hours.
Job Duties & Responsibilities:
• Manage the status of open tickets and complete technical and operational tasks to resolve issues and close tickets efficiently and correctly
• 85% Utilization required
• Work tickets based on SLA priority and expiration and update tickets daily
• Account for time by function and by the customer in ConnectWise with a high level of accuracy to include notes detailing customer work status to ensure continuity of service
• Completion of any other ticket classifications in adherence with the ITIL system of Service Delivery (Type, Subtype, Item, as needed)
• Deliver outstanding customer service on-site and remotely to customers
• Communicate progress and advise customers on account status, and changes, make suggestions, serve as coach regarding products/services and through processes
• Communicate and strategize with POD members on high-priority problems, critical status changes, customer matters, call aging, and training
• Based on customer and company needs, work after hours and on weekends for scheduled projects and in emergency situations
• Configuration, deployment, and installation of end-user workstations and client computers.
• Installation of hardware to desktops, laptops, and servers. Including ram, storage drive, and graphics upgrades.
• Preparing and staging network devices (servers, switches, firewalls) for remote configuration
• Create and Maintain client standard operating procedures for computer builds.
Qualifications:
• College Degree not required, on the job technical support experience preferred
• Minimum 1 year of experience in a closely related position doing MSP technical support/troubleshooting is preferred but not required 
• Familiarity with Microsoft Exchange, SQL, Sharepoint, Windows Server, ConnectWise, and Remote Desktop Services preferred
• Experience in LAN/WAN and basic network troubleshooting
• Experience with Kaseya is a plus
• Experience with Imaging Solutions is a plus (MDT, WDS, ETC)
• Experience with Microsoft Active Directory and Azure AD
• Understanding of Windows and Mac Operating systems, including but not limited to reinstallation of operating systems.
• Microsoft Office Suite, Office 365, Azure, and Windows Operating Systems (Desktop and Server)

job_title: Lead Commissioning Engineer | Utility-Scale Solar | Northeast
job_description:
Lead Commissioning Engineer | Utility-Scale Solar | Northeast

The Company:

We have partnered with an innovative renewable energy services company specializing in commissioning, testing, and operations & maintenance for community and utility-scale solar projects across the United States. Based in Portland, Maine, they are experiencing rapid growth and are building a team of highly skilled engineers to support the delivery of high-quality commissioning services for some of the country's largest solar assets. This is an opportunity to join an entrepreneurial business at an exciting stage of its growth while playing a key role in the clean energy transition.

The Role:

The Lead Commissioning Engineer will serve as the senior technical authority across utility-scale solar commissioning projects, leading testing, commissioning, and energization activities from project completion through commercial operation. This individual will oversee junior engineers and field technicians while working closely with EPC contractors, utilities, OEMs, and project stakeholders to ensure projects are delivered safely, efficiently, and in accordance with engineering specifications.

Key Responsibilities:

Commissioning & Testing:
• Lead commissioning activities for utility-scale solar PV projects, including inverters, transformers, switchgear, SCADA, DAS, trackers, substations, and interconnection equipment,
• Develop detailed commissioning plans, testing procedures, and project-specific acceptance criteria from IFC drawings and engineering documentation,
• Perform and oversee electrical testing, protection relay testing, inverter commissioning, medium-voltage equipment testing, and functional system verification,
• Support project energization, utility witness testing, and commercial operation by acting as the lead technical representative onsite,
• Identify technical issues, troubleshoot system deficiencies, and coordinate corrective actions with EPC contractors, OEMs, and project teams,
• Prepare commissioning reports, testing documentation, punch lists, and as-built records throughout the project lifecycle.

Technical Leadership:
• Provide technical leadership, mentoring, and oversight to commissioning engineers and field technicians,
• Ensure all commissioning activities comply with OSHA regulations, NFPA 70E electrical safety standards, and client requirements,
• Communicate project risks, schedule impacts, and technical updates to Project Managers and senior leadership,
• Support the company's growing Operations & Maintenance business by assisting with troubleshooting, maintenance recommendations, annual inspections, and O&M proposals.

Requirements:
• Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Power Systems, or a related discipline (or equivalent technical experience),
• 3–7+ years of experience commissioning utility-scale solar, renewable energy, or power generation projects,
• Strong knowledge of AC/DC electrical systems, protection relays, SCADA, DAS, inverter technologies, and medium-voltage equipment,
• Experience with electrical testing equipment including Omicron, Doble, or equivalent relay testing platforms,
• Working knowledge of NEC, IEEE 1547, NFPA 70E, IEC, and utility interconnection standards,
• Ability to interpret single-line diagrams, protection studies, and electrical design documentation,
• Professional Engineer (PE) license preferred, or willingness to obtain multi-state licensure,
• Ability to travel approximately 50–75% across project sites throughout the Northeast, Mid-Atlantic, Midwest, and Texas,
• Strong communication, leadership, and documentation skills with proficiency in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and Procore.

job_description:
On-Board Services is hiring a Life Safety Systems / Fire Alarm Designer!

Subject Line: Position Title and State you are Located

Position Type: Full Time / Contract
Job Location: Remote or Hybrid, depending on candidate location
Benefits: Health Insurance, Life Insurance, 401k

Overview/Summary: This job opportunity is available at ANY location in the United States. Ideally, selected candidates will be near one of our client's 140+ U.S. based offices, but we intend to hire the "best" candidates.

Our Client is a global professional services firm that provides engineering, design, construction, and consulting solutions across infrastructure, advanced manufacturing, energy, water, and government sectors.

As a Life Safety Systems / Fire Alarm Designer, you will work on various projects assigned by a Department Manager or Lead Engineer. This position will play a role in the entire design process including but not limited to:
• concept creation
• leading BIM designs with support from your leads and SMEs
• drawing and specification production
• customer and vendor accessibility.
You'll use your skills and experience to provide code-compliant asset protection and fire mitigation and life safety solutions. These solutions include fire alarm, fire detection, fire suppression, gas detection, life safety considerations, smoke modeling, and explosion prevention. Utilizing Revit MEP, our teams create 3D models in coordination with architects and engineers. From those 3D BIM models you'll design fire alarm systems, fire sprinkler systems, smoke and leak detection, security and intrusion detection systems, and more. Using the various applicable Life Safety & Fire codes as well as industry standards you will provide calculations; equipment size and quantities; equipment and construction specifications; network diagrams and various construction deliverables in both 2D and 3D platforms for a constructible design.

Primary Qualifications:
• At least 4 years of Autodesk Revit design experience
• Experience with AutoCAD, Excel, and Revit add-ins
• Experience with Bluebeam Revu
• Familiarity with clash detection and how to resolve issues in ACC/Forma
• Knowledge and design experience with Fire Alarm Systems and related manufacturers' equipment, including VESDA systems
• Knowledge of general design and construction specifications

Preferred Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree in engineering
• Knowledge of IBC, IFC, NFPA101 Life Safety Code and NFPA 72 Fire Alarm and Signaling Code
• Knowledge and application of NFPA 70 (NEC) and electrical design requirements
• Knowledge of NFPA 75, NFPA 76, NFPA 2001, and NFPA 13 codes
• Experience with VESDA ASPIRE or similar software
• Design experience in data centers

In order to be considered for this role, you must be legally authorized to work in the US on a permanent basis without sponsorship. Background screenings required.

What you'll build (Phase 1):

- Product Pipeline: track each product through a 6-stage development workflow plus 3 licensor approval gates, with statuses, specs, and a file/version history.

- Orders & Production: an 8-stage order/production pipeline with inspection results and a bookings/logistics section.

- Factory Portal: each factory logs in and sees ONLY their own products, specs, and files; can update status and ask questions per product; never sees pricing, customers, or other factories (enforced server-side).

- Plus: reflect task/approval status from Wrike (read-only), link files from Dropbox, and a basic natural-language product search.

Requirements:

- Proven full-stack development of custom web apps with logins, role-based access, and APIs (not template/WordPress-only sites). Portfolio required.

- Clean Git and handover practices; transfer all code and accounts to us.

- A daily overlap window with US Eastern time.

- Comfortable integrating third-party APIs (Wrike, Dropbox).

Engagement: fixed-price, milestone-based (5 milestones). We'll begin with a small paid test task before awarding the full project.

To apply:

1. Start your application with the word GBWORLD (so we know you read this).

2. Share 1-2 examples of custom web apps you built with logins and roles (links).

3. Tell us your tech stack and how you'd handle the factory portal's per-factory access control.

5. Confirm you'll hand over all code, accounts, and documentation at the end.

job_title: Remote App Developer jobs – Full‑Time Senior Mobile Engineer (iOS/Android) – $120k‑$150k – Windsor town, Colorado – React Native, Kotlin, Swift, AWS Amplify
job_description: TITLE Remote App Developer jobs – Full‑Time Senior Mobile Engineer (iOS/Android) – $120k‑$150k – Windsor town, Colorado – React Native, Kotlin, Swift, AWS Amplify --- Who we are We’re a midsized SaaS company that grew from a garage project to a platform serving more than 1.5 million monthly active users. Our headquarters live in Windsor town, Colorado, but the product team has been fully distributed for the past three years. We don’t call ourselves “fast‑growing” in a press release; we simply have 12 engineers, three product managers, and a design lead who all meet on Zoom three times a week to review the same backlog we’ve been iterating on since day one. Why this role exists now In Q3 we released version 5.0 of our flagship mobile experience, which added a real‑time collaboration feature that doubled the average session length for users in Windsor town, Colorado. The new feature also introduced a handful of performance regressions that our monitoring tools flagged in the last sprint. We need a senior mobile engineer who can own the end‑to‑end health of our iOS and Android codebases while keeping the release cadence at a predictable four‑week sprint cycle. What you’ll be doing – day in the life - Own a slice of the product – You’ll be the go‑to person for everything related to push notifications and in‑app messaging. That means designing the data flow through AWS Amplify, writing GraphQL mutations, and making sure the delivery SLA stays at 99.9 % for users in Windsor town, Colorado and beyond. - Ship code fast, ship it right – Our CI pipeline runs on CircleCI and uses Docker containers to build both the iOS and Android binaries. You’ll write the pipeline scripts, add unit‑test coverage targets (we run at 85 % on average), and approve pull requests that meet the checklist we’ve honed over the last 18 months. - Collaborate with design – The design lead works with Figma files that they hand off to the dev team. You’ll translate those prototypes into native components using SwiftUI for iOS and Jetpack Compose for Android, while also maintaining a shared React‑Native bridge for cross‑platform screens. - Mentor junior engineers – Two of our developers are on a junior track. You’ll pair‑program with them during the first two weeks of each sprint, run a short “tech talk” on topics like “Effective Use of Firebase Crashlytics” and leave code reviews that focus on readability over cleverness. - Monitor production health – Our dashboards in Datadog and Firebase show a crash‑free rate of 96 % across all devices. You’ll set up alerts for any dip below 95 % and dive into logs with Postman and the AWS CloudWatch console to trace the root cause. Our stack (you’ll be comfortable with at least 8 of these) - Languages & frameworks Swift (5.7+), Kotlin (1.8+), React Native (0.71+), SwiftUI, Jetpack Compose - Backend integration GraphQL (Apollo), REST (Axios), AWS Amplify, Firebase (Auth, Firestore, Crashlytics) - CI/CD CircleCI, Docker, Fastlane for iOS, Gradle for Android - Version control & collaboration Bitbucket, GitHub, JIRA, Confluence - Design hand‑off Figma, Zeplin (occasionally) - Testing XCTest, Espresso, Detox, Jest for React Native unit tests What success looks like - Performance Reduce the average app start‑up time from 1.8 seconds to under 1.2 seconds for 80 % of devices in Windsor town, Colorado within the first two quarters. - Reliability Keep the push‑notification SLA at 99.95 % and maintain a crash‑free rate above 97 % for the next six releases. - Feature velocity Deliver at least two major feature toggles per sprint cycle, each with full test coverage and documentation in Confluence. - Team health Junior engineers report a 30 % increase in confidence scores on the quarterly survey after your mentorship sessions. Who you are - Experience 5‑7 years building production mobile apps, with at least 3 years of shipping iOS or Android releases to the App Store or Google Play. You have a portfolio of apps that have reached at least 10,000 downloads each. - Technical depth You can write a performant list view in SwiftUI, set up a GraphQL subscription, and debug a memory leak using Instruments in less than an hour. - Product mindset You ask “how does this affect the user in Windsor town, Colorado?” before you write code, and you’re comfortable estimating effort in story points and communicating trade‑offs to product managers. - Collaboration style You thrive in a remote environment, have a reliable internet connection, and block out a consistent time slot (e.g., 9 am–12 pm Windsor town time) for daily stand‑ups and pair‑programming. - Culture fit You enjoy celebrating small wins—like a “no‑crash” day—and you’re not afraid to call out a rushed release if the quality could suffer.
