job_title: Sr Contact Center Performance Development Designer - Remote
job_description:
Join the People Helping People

Velera is the nation’s premier payments credit union service organization (CUSO) and an integrated fintech solutions provider. The company serves more than 4,000 financial institutions throughout North America, operating with velocity to help our clients keep pace with the rapid momentum of change and fuel growth in the new era of financial services. Our purpose: We accelerate partners’ success through innovative financial technology solutions and inspired service.

The Opportunity:

The Senior CC Performance Development Designer assists the Strategic Leader of Development with creating content that encourages continued learning and measured performance improvement. We are seeking a dynamic and innovative Contact Center Operations Performance Improvement Designer to join our team. The primary responsibility of this role is to identify knowledge gaps within the operations team and design, develop, and implement impactful and measurable training solutions. Incumbent will assist with the supervision of staff to ensure that company standards are met, and the company policies and procedures are followed while ensuring customer satisfaction. The incumbent will be directly involved with management to deliver coaching and counseling to representatives. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in adult learning instructional design, a keen understanding of operational processes, and a passion for driving employee development and operational excellence.

Day in the Life:

•
Collaborate with subject matter experts (SMEs) to gather and analyze content requirements, ensuring the accuracy and relevance of training materials.

•
Utilize various instructional design methodologies to create impactful learning experiences that cater to diverse learning styles and needs.

•
Design and develop engaging and effective training programs, including gaming modules, instructor-led training, and self-paced learning resources.

•
Understand ratio of daily work volumes and adjust priorities with staff as needed to meet daily and monthly service level goals.

•
Work with leadership to identify and implement process improvements to maximize quality, efficiency, and cost effectiveness of team; facilitate ongoing process improvements.

•
Develop and implement training strategies that align with business goals, aiming to enhance employee performance and operational efficiency.

•
Utilize operation reports to gauge effectiveness of learning solutions and identify areas for improvement, ensuring that training solutions are impactful and measurable.

•
Create user-friendly job aids and reference guides to support ongoing employee development and performance improvement.

•
Partner with supervisors to strengthen employee performance, to include coaching, communicating employee progress toward goals, and report observable behavior patterns to supervisor.

•
Track and document Representative performance, and provide feedback on daily work, service levels, and applicable performance metrics.

•
Inspire and build team commitment by recognizing, rewarding, and encouraging successful performance through ongoing monitoring and hands on coaching.

•
Review Representative errors and conduct call calibrations to provide feedback and coaching.

•
Foster a culture of continuous learning and development by providing coaching, mentoring, and support to employees to encourage employee retention.

•
Communicate effectively with stakeholders to gather feedback, share progress, obtain buy-on, and implement necessary changes to training initiatives.

•
Assist leadership and supporting departments to utilize the learning platform reports to gain insight into their team’s performance.

•
Hold demonstrations of the learning platform and reporting data.

•
Provide input to supervisors for representative’s quarterly performance review and participate in the quarterly review process when needed.

•
Leverage record management system to archive and track training programs.

•
Perform other duties as assigned.

Education:

•
High School Diploma or G.E.D. required.

•
Degree in Instructional Design, Education, Training and Development, or a related field preferred

•
Certification in instructional design or related field is a plus.

Experience:

•
Minimum three (3) years customer service required; preferably in the credit card/bill pay/branch services/lending industry.

•
Strong design experience required.

•
Proven experience in designing curriculum, preferably within an operational or corporate setting.

Knowledge, Skills, & Abilities:

•
Good verbal communication skills.

•
Strong written skills.

•
Analytical and problem-solving abilities.

•
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a diverse environment.

•
Ability to manage multiple projects, work in fast-paced environment, and meet deadlines

•
Demonstrated excellent analytical and quantitative skills

•
Strong understanding of adult learning principles and instructional design methodologies.

•
Proficiency in e-learning authoring or gaming tools (e.g., Articulate, Captivate or Lemonade)

•
Excellent project management skills with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.

Additional Essential Functions and Responsibilities:

•
Regular and reliable attendance.

•
Responsible for reporting risks that are identified to the appropriate team and/or management.

•
Additionally, responsible for managing, monitoring and reporting risks within the scope of your work area, to include, but not limited to Information Security risks.

•
Actively supports and embraces Velera's core values of Dedication, Collaboration, Belonging, Curiosity and Integrity.

Service Standards:

•
Responsible for ensuring that exceptional service is delivered to both internal and external clients by doing what’s right for credit unions.

•
Demonstrate enthusiastic support of corporate mission, vision, core values and long-term business objectives.

•
Support a workplace where every employee feels valued, respected, and connected.

•
Perform job duties within the company’s policies, procedures, service standards and the state and federal laws.

Physical Demands:

•
While performing the duties of this Job, the employee is regularly required to sit; use hands to finger, handle, or feel and talk or hear

•
Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision

•
Ability to occasionally lift/move up to 25 pounds

•
Individuals with a disability who are otherwise able to perform the essential functions of the job may request a reasonable accommodation through the Human Resources department.

At Velera, inclusion isn’t an initiative – it’s how we work. Guided by a people‑helping‑people philosophy, we cultivate a culture where every employee feels valued, respected and empowered to do their best work. We’re committed to building a diverse workforce and fostering meaningful connection across our teams. Through a remote‑first, flexible environment, we prioritize psychological safety, wellbeing and belonging so individuals and teams can collaborate to thrive. Together, we’re shaping a new era of secure, innovative solutions for the clients and communities we serve. Learn more about what it’s like to work at Velera.

Job description:

Job ID: 114544

The Leonardo DRS Naval Power Systems business is a trusted provider of innovative naval gas and hybrid power systems, control technology and support solutions for the U.S. Navy and navies around the world.
Job Summary

We are seeking a highly skilled and forward-thinking Principal Engineer-Commercial Nuclear to join our engineering organization. In this role, you will serve as a technical leader within our commercial nuclear team, driving the design, development, and integration of digital and analog electronic systems. Your expertise will shape innovative solutions and ensure the performance, reliability, and sustainability of mission-critical systems, while contributing to the long-term evolution of our engineering capabilities.
Job Responsibilities
• Design, troubleshoot and implement digital and analog systems incorporating A/D, D/A, microprocessors and microcontrollers
• Provide obsolescence management for legacy analog systems, including the selection and justification of alternate/substitute components
• Generate proposals related to upgrades of existing installed legacy systems as well as new system installations. This includes both technical descriptions and budget/schedule estimates
• Provide schedule, and technical compliance updates to management to effectively manage the execution of complex design programs.
• Validate system performance using analysis, simulation, and laboratory testing.
• Generate complete system documentation and provide input to technical proposals to effectively communicate the design and/or approach to our internal and external customers.
• Apply Design for X (DfX) methodologies including but not limited to Design for Cost, Design for Test, and Design for Manufacturability into the products being developed.
• Independently develop and implement corrective action to product and procedural problems identified.
• Mentor and provide technical guidance to junior engineers, fostering a culture of innovation and excellence.
• Communicate with the customer and internal management as a technical expert to achieve cost effective, technically compliant best in class design solutions.
• Drive continuous improvement initiatives related to power electronics design processes and methodologies.
Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering
• Minimum of 8 years of experience in electronic system design and development
• Experience with a range of technologies, including processor architectures (e.g., Motorola 68000/MC68360, PowerPC, TI DSP), digital logic (TTL/HC), FPGA/toolchains (Xilinx ISE, Altera, Actel/Microsemi SmartFusion with Libero), and interfaces (PCI/PCIe, VME64x, ECC memory)
• Proficiency with design tools such as Cadence OrCAD and Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Visio, PowerPoint)
• Strong product development experience with demonstrated problem-solving and troubleshooting capabilities
• Proven ability to collaborate effectively within cross-functional engineering teams
• Demonstrated leadership experience guiding technical teams and projects
• Experience designing Single Event Upset (SEU)-tolerant systems
• Familiarity with U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) regulations, policies, and procedures
• Experience with Commercial Grade Item Dedication (CGID) for nuclear safety applications
• Knowledge of commercial nuclear and/or military specifications and design practices

U.S. Citizenship required. This position requires the ability to obtain such clearance within a reasonable time after commencement of employment.

Role Summary

This includes unified communications, video conferencing, and collaboration tools for Cisco, Avaya, GrandStream and any other potential voice system, ensuring seamless connectivity, security, and performance across all environments in addition to other IT solutions offered by SamaWave Solutions.

This role requires a mix of technical expertise, project management skills, and customer service excellence, with the ability to translate business needs into effective IT solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Collaboration & Unified Communication

Systems Integration & Support

• Work closely with other teams to ensure seamless system and application integration.

Project Management & Client Engagement

Role Description

We are looking for a QA Engineer to manually test productivity desktop applications built on .NET across Windows and macOS platforms. You will work closely with product and engineering teams to ensure a high-quality experience for an AI-powered desktop product operating in a fast-paced, product-driven environment. This role requires strong cross-platform testing expertise, attention to detail, and the ability to translate product behavior into structured, actionable test cases and bug reports.

Key Responsibilities

•
Perform manual functional testing of desktop applications across Windows and macOS

•
Validate application behavior on Windows 10/11 and macOS (Intel and Apple Silicon/ARM)

•
Design and author structured test cases from product specifications, UI walkthroughs, and exploratory testing

•
Maintain test cases in structured formats (CSV/spreadsheets) with clear steps, expected outcomes, and pass/fail criteria

•
Review and refine AI-generated test plans by removing redundancies, identifying edge cases, and improving clarity and coverage

•
Identify, document, and report bugs with precise reproduction steps, severity classification, screenshots, and logs

•
Test complex desktop UI flows including multi-window interfaces, drag-and-drop, system tray/menu bar interactions, modal dialogs, and installer flows

•
Validate platform-specific behaviors including Windows Shell extensions, registry-related behavior, NSIS installers, macOS app bundles, Info.plist configurations, and file associations

•
Test integrations with Microsoft 365 applications, including Outlook Classic and modern Outlook

•
Collaborate with engineering and product teams using Microsoft Teams and GitLab for issue tracking and communication

Qualifications

•
3+ years of experience in manual functional testing of desktop applications

•
Hands-on experience testing on both Windows and macOS environments (not limited to one platform)

•
Ability to test across Windows 10/11 and macOS (including Intel and Apple Silicon systems)

•
Strong experience in test case design and documentation from product requirements or exploratory testing

•
Experience refining and improving test plans, including AI-generated test cases

•
Strong bug reporting skills with clear, structured, and reproducible documentation

•
Familiarity with complex desktop UI patterns and system-level behaviors

•
Understanding of platform-specific components such as Windows Shell, registry, NSIS installers, macOS app bundles, and system integrations

•
Experience working with Microsoft Teams and Git-based issue tracking systems

•
Strong written English communication skills

Good-To-Have Requirements

•
Experience testing Microsoft Entra ID / OAuth authentication flows

•
Experience with automation testing i.e. Selenium etc.

•
Exposure to AI or LLM-based chat applications

•
Experience validating installers (NSIS on Windows, .dmg/.app on macOS)

•
Experience working in fast-paced startup or product-driven environments

About RB Labs

RB Labs is a Switzerland-based AI and robotics company building the future of intelligent automation. We develop advanced artificial intelligence, robotics, and automation technologies that bridge the gap between digital intelligence and physical systems.

Our mission is to create next-generation robotic solutions capable of operating in real-world environments through the integration of AI, robotics, simulation, and autonomous decision-making. We are a fast-growing team of engineers, researchers, and innovators working on cutting-edge technologies that will shape the future of human-robot interaction.

As we continue to expand our robotics initiatives, we are looking for talented engineers who are passionate about designing and building the hardware that powers the next generation of intelligent robots.

About the Role

We are seeking a Mechanical Design Engineer to design and develop mechanical systems for advanced robotic platforms. The ideal candidate will have strong experience in mechanical product development, CAD design, prototyping, and manufacturing processes.

You will collaborate closely with robotics, AI, electrical, and software engineers to transform concepts into production-ready robotic systems. This role offers the opportunity to work on innovative technologies and contribute directly to the development of real-world robotic products.

Responsibilities

• Design mechanical components, assemblies, and complete robotic systems from concept to production.

• Create detailed 3D CAD models, technical drawings, and manufacturing documentation.

• Design mechanisms for robotic motion, actuation, structural support, and enclosure systems.

• Perform tolerance analysis, design validation, and design-for-manufacturing (DFM) reviews.

• Collaborate with cross-functional engineering teams to integrate mechanical, electrical, and software systems.

• Support prototyping, testing, debugging, and design iteration activities.

• Work with suppliers and manufacturing partners to ensure high-quality production-ready designs.

• Optimize designs for performance, reliability, weight, manufacturability, and cost.

Requirements

• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics, Robotics, or a related field.

• 4+ years of professional experience in mechanical design, product development, or robotics hardware engineering.

• Advanced proficiency in SolidWorks.

• Strong experience with AutoCAD.

• Strong understanding of mechanical design principles, materials, manufacturing processes, and assembly methods.

• Experience designing precision mechanical systems and moving assemblies.

• Knowledge of CNC machining, sheet metal fabrication, injection molding, and 3D printing.

• Experience creating manufacturing drawings and documentation.

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

• Ability to work independently and effectively in a remote environment.

Preferred Qualifications

• Experience designing robotic systems, humanoid robots, robotic arms, mobile robots, or electromechanical products.

• Familiarity with Blender for concept visualization, rendering, or mesh preparation.

• Experience with FEA and engineering simulation tools.

• Understanding of motors, actuators, gearboxes, bearings, and motion transmission systems.

• Knowledge of mechatronics and integration with sensors and electronics.

• Experience taking products from prototype through manufacturing.

• Experience working in startup environments or fast-paced R&D teams.

What We Offer

• Fully remote work environment.

• Opportunity to work on cutting-edge robotics and AI technologies.

• Flexible working arrangements.

• International team of experienced engineers and researchers.

• Competitive compensation package.

• Opportunity to contribute to products that will define the future of robotics.

How to Apply

Email: muntasir@rblabs.ai
• Please submit your resume, portfolio, GitHub (if applicable), and examples of mechanical designs or products you have developed. Candidates with robotics-related project experience are highly encouraged to apply.

Title: BMS Controls Engineer REMOTE

Location: Atlanta United States

Job Description:

Job Title: BMS Controls Engineer

Job Description

We are seeking a BMS/EPMS Development Engineer focused on building and supporting SCADA systems for mission-critical infrastructure such as data centers and critical utilities. This role is heavily centered on remote system development, graphics engineering, and controls integration. The primary focus is on Niagara 4 (N4) HMI/GUI development, Honeywell SCADA/BMS system configuration and integration, and programming systems that monitor various environmental and process variables.

Responsibilities
• Design, develop, and standardize Niagara 4 (N4) HMI screens, dashboards, and navigation structures.
• Build operator-focused interfaces including equipment views, system dashboards, alarm views, trend visualizations, and diagnostic interfaces.
• Implement consistent standards for naming conventions, tagging, graphics templates, and UX flow.
• Configure and support Honeywell SCADA/BMS platforms, including alarm configuration, historical trending, and system-level visualization.
• Integrate Niagara front-end with underlying control systems and supervisory data layers.
• Modify, configure, and troubleshoot PLC/control logic for HVAC systems, cooling infrastructure, and utility/process systems.
• Ensure accurate system representation of field instrumentation such as temperature sensors, pressure transmitters, and control valves.
• Perform point mapping, tagging, and database structuring between field devices and SCADA/BMS platforms.
• Configure communication across protocols such as BACnet/IP, BACnet MS/TP, and Modbus TCP/RTU.
• Execute and support system validation, trend analysis, alarm verification, and remote troubleshooting.
• Support commissioning teams remotely by validating graphics, data accuracy, and system responses.

Work Environment

This is a remote position with a primary focus on remote development of HMI/SCADA and configuration tasks. Travel is minimal to occasional, as needed for system validation or project kick-off. The work environment involves mission-critical facilities, including data centers and critical utilities, and may involve supporting multiple sites or projects simultaneously.

Job Type & Location

This is a Contract position based out of Atlanta, GA.

Pay and Benefits

The pay range for this position is $45.00 - $65.00/hr.

This is a fully remote position.

Role Description

Planet’s Mission Control is a web-based application that empowers spacecraft operators and analysts to monitor, control, and study our growing fleet of satellites. It is flanked by a number of distributed microservices that handle spacecraft scheduling, tasking, telemetry processing, and similar operational needs. Together, these services automate much of the day-to-day operations of Planet's fleet of satellites.

You’ll be a part of a team that builds, maintains, and operates these tools. Our mission is to offer a seamless interface that enables spacecraft operators, analysts, and engineers at Planet to interact effortlessly with our assets in space. We strive to minimize operational burden, and to automate all aspects of satellite operations. You will be a part of a team that will support, enable and engage with the end users to iterate on these systems. In this role, you will tackle and solve a broad array of engineering problems in a dynamic environment.

This is a full-time, remote position based in the United States or Canada. If located near an office, you are expected to work from that office 3 days per week.

Impact You'll Own

•
Maintain and augment the automation and services ultimately responsible for the operation of Planet’s satellites. This includes but is not limited to:

•
Specify and implement new HTTP APIs and help improve existing ones.

•
Evaluate and choose appropriate software technologies.

•
Design and deploy critical infrastructure.

•
Contribute with a team that values open and honest communication, collaboration, self-learning and initiative to solve problems big and small.

•
Work closely with various teams across Planet, ranging from Satellite and Ground Operations, to Pipeline and Product teams to ensure the satellite constellation meets all of the needs to achieve Planet’s missions.

Qualifications

•
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Aerospace Engineering, or similar.

•
4+ years of relevant work experience.

•
Experience in Python programming.

•
Experience in API design.

•
Experience with automated testing, code review, and documentation.

•
Experience with relational database technologies (e.g., Postgres, MySQL, etc.).

•
Experience with maintaining peer-reviewed code and continuous integration and deployment workflows and tooling like GitLab CI and Docker or similar.

•
Proficient Linux skills.

•
Ability to be a self-starter for solving problems in a dynamic environment.

•
Ability to clearly communicate and collaborate with cross-functional teams.

Requirements

•
Experience building services that leverage cloud-based infrastructure and tooling such as AWS or GCP.

•
Experience maintaining infrastructure with Ansible or similar (e.g., Kubernetes, Terraform).

Position: Low Latency Network Engineer
Location: Remote
Duration: 12 Months Contract
As a Low Latency Network Engineer you''ll provide solutions across the company''s datacenters and global colocation estate across the world encompassing all aspects related to collocation sites: site access, equipment procurement, logistics, capacity management (power, space, servers, network ports, etc.), performance management, service availability, connectivity, compute and market data delivery.
You will architect, engineer and deploy ultra-low latency network infrastructure including overseeing and carrying-out installation, monitoring, maintenance, support, optimization and documentation of all network hardware, security hardware, software and connectivity. You will also get involved with managing\participating in multiple projects and be responsible for configuring / optimizing the network to ensure ultra-low\low latency is achieved for intraday trading and direct market access systems, providing exchange, broker and managed service providers connectivity to support market data delivery and order execution.
Requirements:
• 20+ years of expereince
• Experience with Trade Platforms, specifically Corvil - Corvil is NICE TO HAVE
• Strong knowledge on Electronic and Algo Trading Platforms, Feed Handlers, Order Routers, Smart Order Routers, FIX protocols
• Exchange binary FIX protocols such as OUCH also ITCH.
• Strong Network engineering and project delivery experience
• Advanced knowledge of routing and switching protocols
• Experience with Unicast: EIGRP, OSPF, BGP, NAT, IPSEC and GRE
• Experience with Switching: STP, PC/vPC/MLAG, CDP/LLDP, QoS, HSRP/VRRP, VLAN/VxLAN, Spine and Leaf infrastructures.
• In-depth experience with Cisco Nexus network switching, routing hardware and software
• In-depth experience with Cisco Nexus network switching, routing hardware and software
• Knowledge of network monitoring PCAP decoding tools landscape (Corvil, SFlow, timestamping (PTP) preferred but not required)
• Knowledge of Algorithmic/DMA/HFT and trading platforms and how the trade lifecycle operates
• Solid understanding of market venue, exchange protocols (binary, FIX, ITCH,OUCH, Pillar, etc.) and trading systems across multiple asset classes
• Strong scripting and automation experience (Python preferred but not required)
• Excellent communication skills; ability to perform under pressure and detail oriented
• While certification are interesting, they are not required as we value experience over certification

Remote

We are looking for a highly creative Visual Designer / Animator to bring ideas to life through motion, effects, and image-based storytelling. This role is focused on developing compelling animation concepts and visual narratives that communicate complex ideas in a simple, engaging way.

Our client, a leading innovator in the technology sector, is seeking a UX Designer to join their dynamic team. As a UX Designer, you will be part of the Experience Strategy & Design Studio supporting large-scale, end-to-end customer experiences. The ideal candidate will demonstrate creativity, strategic thinking, and excellent communication skills, which will align successfully within our collaborative environment.

Location: Remote

What's the Job?

• Conceptualize and deliver design artifacts at various levels of fidelity to support customer experience projects.
• Create visual design documents including storyboards, wireframes, journey maps, personas, and task flows from concept to delivery.

• 3–4+ years of hands-on primary UX research experience, including planning and conducting customer or user interviews.
• Ability to independently gather user insights and apply them to strategic design solutions.
• Experience with strategic design, journey mapping, navigation modeling, and broader experience design work.

What's in it for me?

• Opportunity to work on impactful, large-scale customer experiences with direct engagement from executive leadership.
• Collaborative, fast-paced, and agile work environment fostering innovation and growth.
• Chance to develop and showcase your strategic design and research skills across diverse projects.
• Remote work flexibility with accommodating work hours to suit different time zones.

job_title: FLEX Senior Designer, User Experience & Design
job_description:
This is a temporary position.

With every journey, travel has the power to shape us and our world. Continue your journey as the Senior Designer User Experience & Design at Marriott International, repeatedly voted one of Fortune’s 100 Best Companies to Work For®. Join our high energy and talented User Experience & Design team creating best-in-class digital experiences for one of the most recognized hospitality companies in the world.

This role is responsible for designing commerce-focused web products ranging from near-term tactical to longer-term strategy with a keen focus on data-driven, best-in-class product design. They will contribute to digital experiences that deliver on both revenue conversion and emotional engagement to support business outcomes. Specifically, they will be part of the team designing Marriott’s 9,000+ hotel websites, the online experience of over 9,000 hotels and resorts worldwide. Partnering closely with the Director of User Experience, product management and technology colleagues to meet needs across our business, leisure, member and non-member customers.

This position requires the ability to produce design as an individual contributor and provide direction to internal and external designers.

A strong portfolio is critical. Your portfolio should showcase experience in UX and interaction design in commerce solutions, particularly highlighting work within a multi-brand design system, scalable solutions, bespoke customizations within templated products, simplicity and ease-of-use.

CANDIDATE PROFILE

Education and Experience

· 5+ years of relevant experience in digital product design

· 4-year undergraduate degree, with bachelor’s degree in relevant discipline, or equivalent experience

· Experience in multiple of the following user experience disciplines: interaction design and visual design

· Experience working with a multi-brand design systems and using variables/tokenization

· Strong grounding in the atomic design framework,

· Excellent storyteller, with a strong focus on content within design

· Experience with major, large-scale global digital apps, web sites, or other digital products within the commerce space

· Proven experience delivering concepts, templates, quick, iterative design prototypes across platforms (desktop, mobile web, app, etc.)

You’re exactly who we’re looking for if you:

• Have a strong iterative and problem-solving approach to your work

• Communicate through precise specs, compelling visuals and well documented deliverables

• Detail-oriented and precise

• Comfortable working within a design system, but not constrained by it: pushes for creativity within constraints

• Promote UX best practices drawn from a variety of disciplines (within and beyond hospitality)

• Always up to date on the latest trends and methodologies

• Able to act quickly and independently

• Ability to simultaneously manage multiple projects and work across multi-disciplinary teams

Nice to Have:

· Expertise in large-scale global digital entities or other digital products within the hospitality, or travel industry

· Superior communication and interpersonal skills including discovery facilitation, presentation, and storytelling

· Experience with Agile / SAFE development methodologies

CORE WORK ACTIVITIES

Demonstrating and Applying Discipline Knowledge

• Produces sketches, wireframes, flows, designs, templates, content diagrams, Figma prototypes and/or presentations as needed to communicate experience design

• Provides subject matter expertise on key functional systems to help shepherd strategic initiatives from idea to feasible implementation

• References Design System for appropriate patterns, components, and styles during user experience design work

• Produces both innovative high-level design concepts / strategy visualizations as well as development-ready designs with specifications

• Ensure a quality experience on live digital products by participating in the user acceptance testing process

• Uses software including: Figma; AEM Storybook (or equivalent); Microsoft Excel, Word and PowerPoint

• Keeps skills up-to-date on industry trends and applies new knowledge to job, identifying strategic opportunities for competitive advantages through those findings

• Clearly present your work citing industry best practices, qual and quant data to support your decisions

• Be a thought partner providing recommendations based on research and data for experience enhancements across all digital touchpoints

• Collaborates with a variety of user experience disciplines within the both the UX team, product team, and development teams(Information Architecture, Design System/Standards, User Research, Copywriting, Front-End, Functional, Authoring) to advance your work

Managing Work, Projects, and Policies

• Provides accurate estimates as to needed time to complete work to feed project plans (and therefore personal deadlines)

• Meets deadlines once agreed upon

• Reports impediments to completing work and meeting deadlines

• Provides regular status updates to manager

• Successfully manages multiple assignments at once

• Accepts critique and critiques work of others in a helpful and professional manner

• Communicates in a timely and appropriate manner with teammates

• Manage other individual contributors on the team.

Additional Responsibilities

• Works independently with minimal supervision, but knows when issues should be escalated to manager

• Develops relationships with members of other teams, and leverages those relationships to answer questions

• Presents work with clarity and confidence; Performs other reasonable duties as assigned by manager

At Marriott International, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, welcoming all and providing access to opportunity. We actively foster an environment where the unique backgrounds of our associates are valued and celebrated. Our greatest strength lies in the rich blend of culture, talent, and experiences of our associates.  We are committed to non-discrimination on any protected basis, including disability, veteran status, or other basis protected by applicable law.

All locations offer 401(k) plan, stock purchase plan, discounts at Marriott properties, commuter benefits, employee assistance plan, and childcare discounts. No new H1B sponsorship is available for this role.

However, candidates who are currently on a valid H1B visa and require a transfer are welcome to apply. We will support H1B transfers for qualified candidates.

For every role, a technical coding assessment is mandatory. Please apply only if you are confident in your technical abilities and hands-on experience.

Job Summary

We are seeking an experienced SAP Fiori Developer to design, build, and enhance modern, user-friendly SAP applications that meet enterprise standards for usability, performance, and maintainability. In this role you will own the full Fiori/UI5 development lifecycle, working closely with functional consultants, ABAP developers, and UX teams to deliver responsive Fiori applications across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices. The ideal candidate will combine deep expertise in SAPUI5, Fiori elements, and OData with a strong sense of UX, accessibility, and clean-architecture principles, and will adhere to SAP Fiori Design Guidelines while delivering business value through well-designed user experiences.

Key Responsibilities

• Design and develop SAP Fiori applications using SAPUI5, Fiori elements, and the Fiori Launchpad, ensuring adherence to SAP Fiori Design Guidelines
• Build and consume OData v2/v4 services, including model definitions, navigation properties, expansions, and batch operations
• Implement custom controls, formatters, fragments, and reusable components in line with internal coding standards
• Develop role-based Fiori Launchpad configurations, including catalogs, groups, tiles, and target mappings
• Optimize Fiori applications for performance, including bundling, caching, lazy loading, and reduction of network round trips
• Build adaptive applications using flexibility services and key-user adaptation features of S/4HANA
• Implement strong authentication and authorization patterns aligned with SAP security guidelines
• Integrate Fiori applications with SAP BTP services, including XSUAA, Workflow, and Document Service
• Support deployment of Fiori applications to both ABAP frontend servers and BTP-hosted runtimes
• Collaborate closely with ABAP developers to define backend OData services and CDS views that support efficient Fiori applications
• Conduct thorough functional, regression, and cross-device/cross-browser testing of applications, including edge-case validation and accessibility checks, to ensure consistent user experience across the supported platform matrix
• Provide hypercare and post-go-live support, including issue triage, performance tuning, and continuous improvement
• Maintain comprehensive, current technical documentation — including architecture diagrams, design decisions, configuration references, runbooks, and operational procedures — so that the system remains supportable, auditable, and easy to onboard new engineers onto over time

Required Qualifications

• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical discipline
• Five or more years of SAP Fiori / UI5 development experience
• Strong hands-on experience with SAPUI5, Fiori elements, and the Fiori Launchpad
• Solid experience with OData v2/v4 modeling and consumption
• Hands-on experience with HTML5, JavaScript (ES6+), and CSS
• Working knowledge of ABAP CDS views, BOPF, and gateway service development
• Experience deploying Fiori applications to ABAP frontend and BTP runtimes
• Strong understanding of authentication, authorization, and SAP security patterns
• Excellent attention to UX, accessibility, and performance
• Strong communication and documentation skills

Preferred Qualifications

• SAP certifications in Fiori / UI5 development
• Hands-on experience with SAP Build Apps / Low-Code platforms
• Experience with SAP Mobile Services or hybrid mobile applications
• Familiarity with CI/CD tooling for SAPUI5 (Project Piper, GitHub Actions)
• Exposure to TypeScript-based UI5 development

job_title: Entry Level_Technical Account Manager / Field Applications Engineer – Midwest & Eastern Region 11272
job_description:
Company: A leading, Tokyo-headquartered global manufacturer specializing in high-performance Analog Power Management ICs)

Work Location: 100% Fully Remote (Targeting candidates based in Midwest or East Coast)

Compensation & Benefits:

• Base Salary: $100,000 - $120,000

• Performance Bonus: Target of 20% of base salary

• Health Benefits: Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance (100% company-covered)

• Car Allowance+ 401(k) Match

Position Summary:

Our client is a premier, Tokyo-headquartered global leader specializing in ultra-compact, high-efficiency Analog Power Management ICs (PMICs). As they aggressively expand their direct engineering and commercial footprint in North America, we are seeking a motivated, tech-driven Technical Account Manager / FAE to join as a founding member of their regional team.

We are NOT looking for a traditional, non-technical salesperson. We are hunting for a sharp engineering mind with a passion for analog electronics. If you have a solid foundation in electrical engineering and want to step out of the lab or rigid corporate structures to fast-track your career into high-impact commercial leadership, this role is designed for you. Prior B2B sales experience is NOT required—our client will fully train you on the business side if your technical foundation is rock-solid.

Key Responsibilities:

• Engineer-to-Engineer Consultation: Act as the technical and commercial face of the company, standing toe-to-toe with customer hardware R&D teams (Automotive Tier-1s, IoT, Industrial OEMs) to solve complex power management design challenges.

• Circuit & Layout Analysis: Review customer circuit schematics and PCB layouts to troubleshoot noise (EMI), thermal constraints, and space issues, positioning our proprietary integrated inductor (coil-in-chip) "Micro DC/DC" technology as the ultimate solution.

• Account Ownership: Manage the entire design-in cycle from initial technical discovery and simulation (LTspice/Altium) to final BOM optimization and RFQ/commercial quotation.

• Global Collaboration: Bridge the gap between North American customers and our global design/marketing teams in Japan to define next-generation product requirements.

Requirements (Strictly Enforced):

1. Education :

• Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering (BSEE) or Computer Engineering (BCE) is required.

2. Experience Level:

• Around 3 years of hands-on engineering experience (Post-graduation or through intensive Co-op/internship programs at Tier-1 automotive, industrial, or semiconductor companies).

• We are explicitly targeting highly motivated junior-to-mid-level professionals or recent powerhouse graduates.

3. Required Technical Expertise (Analog & Power Focus):

• Deep theoretical and practical understanding of Analog Circuit Design and Power Electronics fundamentals (Buck/Boost converters, LDOs, Switching Regulators, BMS, or Power Distribution).

• Hands-on experience with hardware validation, electronics troubleshooting, or PCB debug using laboratory equipment (Oscilloscopes, Multimeters, Spectrum Analyzers).

• Familiarity with industry-standard design/simulation tools (such as LTspice, TINA-TI, Altium Designer, KiCad, or Cadence Allegro).

• Knowledge of vehicle network protocols (CAN/LIN) or vehicle-level reliability/noise standards (EMC/EMI mitigation, DFMEA) is highly preferred.

4. Commercial & Interpersonal Attributes (No Prior Sales Title Needed):

• Excellent oral and written communication skills; ability to distill complex abstract engineering data into clear, concise language.

• Prior leadership or public-facing exposure (e.g., IEEE student leadership, University Ambassador, or project management) is a massive plus.

• A strong desire to move into a highly lucrative, high-visibility business development and client-facing career path.

Pay: $100,000.00 - $120,000.00 per year

Benefits:

• 401(k)

• 401(k) matching

• Dental insurance

• Health insurance

• Paid time off

• Vision insurance

• Work from home

Application Question(s):

• Do you have degree in either EE (Electrical Engineering) or CE (Computer Engineering)or a closely related hardware-focused discipline?

Experience:

• power electronics: 1 year (Required)

• Analog Circuit Design: 1 year (Required)

Willingness to travel:

• 50% (Required)

Work Location: Remote

About The Company Optum, a part of UnitedHealth Group, is a leading global organization dedicated to transforming healthcare through innovative technology solutions. Committed to improving health outcomes, Optum leverages advanced data analytics, artificial intelligence, and digital tools to connect individuals with essential care, pharmacy benefits, and health resources.

The company fosters a culture of inclusion, collaboration, and continuous growth, offering comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities to its employees. With a focus on advancing health optimization on a global scale, Optum strives to make healthcare more accessible, efficient, and effective for diverse communities worldwide.

About The Role The Lead AI/ML Engineer will play a pivotal role within Optum’s Value Connect team, which is developing an AI-first platform dedicated to value-based care. This platform utilizes cutting-edge machine learning, large language models, agents, and automation to enhance provider performance, care outcomes, and operational efficiency.

The successful candidate will provide hands-on technical leadership, designing and implementing scalable, production-grade AI/ML solutions that transform complex healthcare data into actionable workflows. This role involves collaborating with cross-functional teams including product managers, data scientists, clinical experts, and business stakeholders to develop innovative AI capabilities such as predictive models, conversational AI assistants, agentic workflows, and MLOps practices. The position offers flexibility to work remotely within the United States, with specific in-office requirements for employees in Minneapolis or Washington, D.C.

Qualifications

• Bachelor’s degree with 6+ years, Master’s degree with 4+ years, or PhD with 3+ years of relevant experience in AI/ML engineering or related fields

• At least 3 years of hands-on experience developing, validating, tuning, and evaluating classical machine learning, deep learning, and Generative AI models

• Minimum of 3 years of professional experience writing production-level code in Python using modern ML/AI libraries

• Experience building and deploying AI systems utilizing RAG frameworks, vector databases, or orchestration libraries such as LangChain or LangGraph

• Proficiency in deploying, operating, and managing MLOps workloads on cloud platforms like Azure or AWS, including containerization

• Strong understanding of software engineering practices including version control (GitHub), CI/CD pipelines, and code reviews

Responsibilities

• Lead the design, architecture, and end-to-end implementation of AI/ML, GenAI, RAG-based, and agentic solutions in healthcare

• Apply advanced machine learning, deep learning, statistical, and Generative AI techniques to solve complex clinical, operational, and financial problems

• Build, validate, optimize, and fine-tune AI/ML models, including performance tuning, quantization, and cost optimization strategies

• Develop and maintain production-grade APIs (e.g., FastAPI, REST) to expose AI capabilities at scale

• Establish and own MLOps practices, including model versioning, lifecycle management, monitoring, logging, and drift detection to ensure reliability

• Deploy AI workloads on cloud platforms such as Azure or AWS using containerization, Databricks, PySpark, and modern data platforms

• Implement engineering best practices for software quality, security, responsible AI, and conduct design and code reviews

• Collaborate with product teams, data scientists, clinical stakeholders, and business units to develop innovative solutions

• Mentor engineering team members and evaluate emerging AI technologies to ensure continuous platform enhancement

• Leverage enterprise-approved AI tools and technologies to automate workflows, streamline development, and improve platform capabilities

Benefits

• Competitive salary ranging from $145,500 to $249,500 annually, based on experience and location

• Comprehensive benefits package including health insurance, dental, and vision coverage

• Incentive and recognition programs to reward performance

• Equity stock purchase options and 401(k) retirement plans

• Flexible remote work arrangements within the United States

• Opportunities for professional growth, training, and career development

job_description:
Title: Network Infrastructure Engineer

Hybrid

Location: Lansing, MI

Job Description:
• Staff positions are supportive roles providing support to our rehabilitation mission. Applicants for staff positions do not need to have a barrier to employment to be eligible for these roles.*
• To ensure full consideration for a staff position, please attach a resume to your application so we can gain a complete view of your experiences*

At Peckham, people matter. A whole lot. Working at Peckham means you’ll find a safe space to grow, the freedom to take risks and an opportunity to succeed. When you choose Peckham, you choose more than a job—you join a mission-driven community that values connection, inclusion and personal growth. Our supportive and flexible environment offers holistic and rewarding work experiences tailored to your needs and emphasizing your wellbeing. Integrated into every role is the opportunity to support Peckham’s mission, make a lasting impact on people, and uplift our community.

A Brief Overview

The Network Infrastructure Engineer provides support to enterprise-level networks as apart of IT Network Infrastructure and Data Center Support. This position focuses on Local Area Networks (LAN). Their responsibilities include the configuration, monitoring, and support of Peckham's enterprise network infrastructure. Additionally, this role is responsible for troubleshooting and resolving any issues that arise such as support tickets and manage incidents, service requests, and problems through their full life cycle.

What you will do

Collaborate with the IT Network Infrastructure and Data Center Support teams to implement and support secure network, wireless, and data/voice communication systems within the Peckham organization.
Apply knowledge of enterprise-level wired and wireless networking.
Stay informed about current network technologies and hardware platforms and actively engage in learning about emerging technologies and trends in the IT field.
Adhere to Peckham's change management and documentation processes.
Update and maintain policies and procedures for the Peckham Information Technology Department to ensure clarity and compliance.
Troubleshoot and resolve any issues that arise. Support tickets and manage incidents, service requests, and problems through their full life cycle.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to assess network requirements and develop solutions that enhance connectivity and efficiency.
Document network configurations, processes, and procedures to ensure knowledge transfer and compliance with best practices.
Perform routine audits of systems and software
Participate in after-hours support and project work along with on-call rotations as necessary and assigned.
Participate in, and support, Peckham Information Technology Department projects and initiatives with fellow Information Technology staff.

What you will Need

Bachelors in Information Technology or a related field or an equivalent combination of education, industry certifications, and experience
3 - 5 years experience in an Information Technology field
1 - 3 years of progressive experience with enterprise local area networking technologies.
1 - 3 years hands-on experience with Cisco network routers, routing/switching protocols, network switch, and Cisco wireless technologies
Possess an understanding and hands-on experience with physical network routers (routing protocols) and network switch technologies
Analytical and troubleshooting skills to diagnose and resolve network issues effectively
Proficient in using network monitoring and management tools to ensure optimal performance and security
Excellent verbal and written communication skills to convey technical information to non-technical stakeholders
Strong attention to detail to ensure accuracy in network configurations and documentation
Able to accommodate a flexible work schedule, including nights and weekends

1 - 3 years of years programing experience (Python + YANG/JSON/YAML)

Summary:

• The OMS Platform Reliability Lead is a highly technical role responsible for the health, stability, and automated evolution of the Fluent Commerce Order Management ecosystem.
• Unlike a traditional operations role, this position leans heavily into Systems Engineering, requiring the ability to read and debug Java extensions, design complex GraphQL mutations, and build automated remediation tools for the "RUN" team.
• Role will manage the technical RUN support team and serve as the bridge between software engineering and IT operations.
• Primary focus will be to transition from manual support to "Self-Healing" operations by implementing automation for order replays, data deduplication, and predictive alerting.

Key Responsibilities:

• Design and implement automated "Order Replay" mechanisms within Fluent Commerce to resolve synchronization failures between event-driven integrations without manual intervention.
• Build advanced telemetry dashboards (using tools like Splunk, Datadog, or New Relic) to monitor GraphQL query performance, API latency, and webhook success rates.
• Design and tune threshold-based alerting for the RUN team to identify "Stuck Orders" or inventory mismatches before they impact the customer experience.
• Script custom utilities using the Fluent Commerce SDK or REST APIs to facilitate bulk updates and system cleanups.
• Act as the ultimate technical escalation point for incidents requiring code-level analysis of Java custom extensions or complex GraphQL mutations.
• Lead technical Root Cause Analysis (RCA) by performing deep-dives into application logs and event-driven architecture to identify architectural bottlenecks.
• Analyze API response times and database interaction patterns to propose platform optimizations to the development team.
• Oversee the incident management lifecycle, ensuring documentation includes code-level workarounds and technical "bug-fixes" for future reference.
• Serve as the primary technical point of contact for E-commerce and architecture teams to ensure operational requirements are included in the dev roadmap.
• Collaborate with Fluent Commerce product engineers to align on platform upgrades and API versioning impacts.
• Mentor the RUN support team in technical skills including GraphQL query optimization and Java debugging.
• Validate technical configurations and platform extensions during the release cycle to ensure deployment integrity and performance stability.
• Manage version control using GIT, ensuring proper branching strategies for operational hotfixes and configuration changes.

Requirements

• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
• 5+ years in OMS Technical Operations or Platform Engineering, with specific experience in high-volume, event-driven SaaS environments.
• Advanced technical knowledge of Fluent Commerce (specifically Webhooks, Essential Rules, and the Fluent GraphQL API) highly preferred
• Proficiency in reading, debugging, and identifying performance issues in custom Java extensions.
• Expert proficiency in query/mutation design, including the use of aliases, fragments, and variables for complex data manipulation.
• Comprehensive understanding of RESTful architectures, JSON schemas, and event-driven patterns (Pub/Sub, Kafka, or Event Grid).
• Experience with monitoring tools such as Datadog, Splunk, ELK Stack, or New Relic.
• Deep experience with repository management and deployment pipelines.
• Ability to explain a "race condition" or "API timeout" to a business stakeholder in terms of revenue and customer impact.

Who We Are:

The Fountain Group is a nationwide staffing firm with over 80 Fortune 100-500 clients. Since 2001, TFG has maintained a consistent standard of excellence, and our work is broadly recognized every year through numerous industry performance awards. Our success is a team effort.

We are building a modern, incredibly cool-looking mobile experience. What we need is a visual savant—someone who obsesses over pixel-perfect layouts, stunning interfaces, and fluid motion that makes an app feel alive."

ABOUT BARKIE.AI

Barkie is an AI-native golf platform changing how the game is played, tracked, and experienced. We're a Google Early Access Partner and USGA affiliate building two flagship products: ScoreShot (photo-to-scorecard digitization) and Broadie, a voice-first AI caddie with GPS, strokes gained analytics, and a real-money gambling engine. We move fast, ship real features to real users, and care deeply about the quality of what we build.

THE ROLE

We aren't looking for a generic wireframer. We need a visual heavy-hitter to define the aesthetic future of Barkie. You will partner closely with me (our UX designer) to take structural frameworks and user flows and turn them into visual masterpieces. You will own the look, feel, and motion of our flagship products, with a primary focus on iOS, followed by matching that premium experience on Android and bringing it seamlessly to smartwatches.

Beyond crafting beautiful screens, a massive part of your role will be architecting and building a scalable design system from scratch to ensure consistency as we grow.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

→ Partner hand-in-hand with the UX Designer to translate wireframes, user flows, and architecture into stunning, high-fidelity interfaces.

→ Build a scalable design system from the ground up in Figma, ensuring components, typography, grids, and tokens are organized for effortless engineering handoff.

→ Establish a modern, premium visual direction for our mobile and wearable apps.

→ Design micro-interactions and create prototypes (Figma, AI tools, or motion suites) that clearly dictate how elements move and feel.

→ Translate voice-first and data-heavy features into clean, intuitive, and beautiful watch face interfaces across iOS and Android wearables.

→ Collaborate with our frontend engineering team to ensure your visual vision is executed perfectly down to the pixel.

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

→ Elite Visual Design Skills: A portfolio that proves you can build interfaces that look modern, sleek, and high-end.

→ Design System Architecture: Experience building, maintaining, and scaling comprehensive mobile design systems.

→ Prototyping Chops: Mastery of Figma (or cutting-edge AI prototyping tools). You don't just hand off static screens; your prototypes clearly demonstrate interaction, transition, and flow.

→ Mobile & Wearable Native: Deep experience designing for iOS (main focus) and Android. Bonus points if you’ve shipped sleek, functional designs for Apple Watch or Wear OS.

→ Great Collaborative Chemistry: You love bouncing ideas back and forth. You respect the structural research and user psychology of UX, and want to elevate it visually.

→ Startup Speed: Comfort moving fast, iterating in ambiguity, and bringing creative ideas to the table.

→ Bonus: You play golf, or at least appreciate why people are obsessed with it.

WHAT WE OFFER

→ Meaningful equity stake in a well-positioned, early-stage company

→ Google Early Access Partner status — see how your designs interact with unreleased Gemini models before anyone else

→ A chance to build a visual brand that millions of golfers will look at every single weekend

→ Direct founder collaboration — no layers, no noise, no corporate drag

→ Your name on something that matters in a sport people love

If you care about craft and want to build the coolest looking app at the intersection of AI, sports, and mobile—send a DM or reach out at hello@barkie.ai.

Portfolio showcasing mobile UI, design system work, and interaction/prototyping is required

job_title: Senior UX Designer (Contract) — Consolidate Multi-Portal Experience + Mobile Apps
job_description:
About Navia Benefit Solutions:
We’re a people first benefit administrator that relies on a fast-growing team of creative thinkers, problem-solvers, and go-getters to bring our participant and client experience to the next level. Our mission? To create better lives and provide a simple and amazing benefits experience. If you love providing excellent service, innovating the way benefits are administered, and being part of an inclusive workplace community, you’ll fit right in.
About the Role:
Navia Benefit Solutions is consolidating multiple legacy portals (FSA, HSA, COBRA, commuter, retirement administration) into a single unified web portal and matching iOS + Android apps. We’re looking for a senior UX designer to lead the design strategy and execute the full visual + interaction design across web and mobile.

The unified experience needs to handle role-based access control (RBAC) — participants, employers, brokers, internal CSRs — through a navigation model that flexes by role without feeling fragmented. The visual system must align with the new corporate website branding and bring a fresh, modern UI that genuinely blends the health and retirement product lines into one coherent product experience.
What You’ll Do:
• Information architecture and navigation design across web + mobile (iOS + Android)
• RBAC permission model translated into navigation, content surfacing, and interaction patterns per user role
• Design system anchored on the new corporate website brand (we’ll provide brand guidelines)
• Unified visual language that fuses our health and retirement product lines without losing either
• Wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs ready for engineering handoff
• Dev-handoff specs (Figma component library, design tokens, redlines)
What We’re Looking For:
• 8+ years senior UX experience, with at least 3 leading platform consolidation or brand-consolidation work end-to-end
• Portfolio includes regulated-industry product design (healthcare, financial services, benefits, retirement, insurance, or similar)
• Strong on both UX strategy and visual execution — not visual-only
• Experienced with RBAC and multi-persona product design
• Comfortable working from existing brand guidelines while bringing original product-level visual decisions
• Strong design-system thinking — Figma component-library fluency expected
• Mobile + web parity experience (iOS, Android, and responsive web)
Engagement
      • Contract, project-based with milestone deliverables
      • Estimated 10-14 weeks
      • Hourly or fixed-fee — flexible based on your preference
      • Remote, with periodic working sessions over Teams
      • Reports to EVP, Product & Engineering
How to Apply:
      •  Portfolio link with 2-3 case studies most analogous to this scope
      •  Short note on your approach to consolidating fragmented product experiences
      •  Availability and rate range
      •  2 references from prior platform-consolidation or design-system engagements
Work Environment:
Candidates within 30 miles of one of our four office locations may be asked to come into the office for a hybrid schedule depending on the role.
Eligible candidates will reside in one of the following states: AZ, CA, CO, FL, GA, IA, IL, IN, KS, MA, ME, MI, MN, MO, MT, NC, ND, NE, NJ, NV, NY, OH, OR, PA, SD, TN, TX, UT, AND WA
Why Join Navia?
• Impact: Play a pivotal role in supporting employers and employees nationwide.
• Growth: Advance your skills with a team committed to excellence and innovation.
• Culture: Join a company that values collaboration, innovation, and customer-first solutions.
Perks and Benefits:
• Competitive Salary Range:
• Generous health benefits
• Company sponsored wellness benefits
• Complimentary Life Insurance and Long-Term Disability Insurance
• Paid time off
• 6 Paid Holidays & 2 Paid Floating Holidays
• Work from home and hybrid schedules available!

Ready to Join Us?
Take the first step toward your new career in creating better lives —apply now!
For any questions or to check on your application, reach out to HR@naviabenefits.com.

Please visit our career page and apply directly: www.naviabenefits.com/workatnavia

job_title: Lead C++ engineer with Multi-threading and Trading Experience
job_description:

This role will be joining an existing team, backend real-time API's for their software solution. The ideal candidate is a self-motivated multitasker with at least 2 years working with C++ in a Linux environment. Ideally, working with FIX or integrations with trading exchanges. You should excel in working with large-scale applications and frameworks, and have outstanding communication.

Responsibilities:
• Writing clean, high-quality, high-performance, maintainable code
• Develop and support software, including applications, database integration, interfaces, and new functionality enhancements
• Support, test, and deployment of new products and features

Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree in Computer Science (or related field)
• Prior experience working on trading or clearing applications
• Financial transaction protocols. You've built systems that speak FIX, FIXML, OM, or similar. Not just read about them, real-time, high-throughput systems. You've built data-intensive systems where correctness and performance are both critical. You think in latency, throughput, and behavior under load.
• Straight-through processing. Hands-on experience with the full lifecycle of transaction ingest to post-trade use cases - - - Systems-level concurrency.

• Streaming infrastructure. Hands-on with Kafka or equivalent at production scale
• Data stores. You have hands-on experience with databases suited to high-volume, high-correctness transaction data at production scale.
• Production instincts. You've been in the hot seat on production data issues. You've diagnosed real-time issues under pressure with incomplete logs and inconsistent inputs, and shipped the fix before market open.

Company Overview: brightfin provides clients with a suite of IT financial management and managed software subscriptions to enable your organization to rationalize, comprehend, normalize, optimize, and allocate spend to clearly show IT’s value in delivering revenue, services, and organizational enablement for the enterprise. Our goal is to shape a world where every technology decision is grounded in financial truth, operational clarity, and intelligent automation — enabling organizations to reduce total spend and reinvest in critical innovation.

The Role:

You will own features end-to-end — from data model to UI — across our ServiceNow-native platform and the decoupled services we are building alongside it. You will pair daily with AI agents to design, build, test, and ship. You will set the bar for what AI-driven development looks like at brightfin.

What you own

• Features end-to-end. Schema, API, UI, tests, observability. We do not split front and back across people — we split it across agents.

• AI-driven development. You orchestrate Claude, Cursor, Codex, and our internal agents to ship faster than a traditional team. You write the prompts, you review the output, you own the result.

• Production code in our platform. JavaScript/TypeScript, Node, React, and Python where the data lives. ServiceNow scoped apps where the integration runs.

• Tests are non-negotiable. Agents help you write them; you own that they actually catch what they should.

• The next pattern. You find a workflow that saves the team a day a week, you document it, you teach it. We learn in public.

What you bring:

Non-negotiables

• Experience shipping production software across the stack.

• Strong judgement and sense of urgency

• Strong TypeScript/JavaScript and modern React. Comfort in Python or another backend language.

• Fluent AI-assisted workflow. You already use Claude, Cursor, Copilot, or equivalent as a primary part of how you build. You can describe specifically how AI changed the way you ship.

• Systems thinking. You design before you code. You can defend a tradeoff in plain language to a non-engineer.

Strongly preferred

• ServiceNow platform experience — scoped apps, GlideRecord, REST integrations, Now Experience UI Framework.

• Experience building or extending AI agents — LangGraph, AutoGen, custom orchestration, or MCP servers.

• Background in data-heavy SaaS — analytics, finance, telecom, or cloud cost management.

• Experience shipping in a PE-backed growth company.

Compensation & Benefits:

• brightfin offers a comprehensive health, dental and vision benefits package.

• Paid time off. We strongly believe in work-life balance and taking time for yourself.

• 401K with employer match

Location: Remote, US Based, Full Time

Physical Requirements: Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About the Role:

Planet’s Mission Control is a web-based application that empowers spacecraft operators and analysts to monitor, control, and study our growing fleet of satellites. It is flanked by a number of distributed microservices that handle spacecraft scheduling, tasking, telemetry processing, and similar operational needs. Together, these services automate much of the day-to-day operations of Planet's fleet of satellites.

You’ll be a part of a team that builds, maintains, and operates these tools. Our mission is to offer a seamless interface that enables spacecraft operators, analysts, and engineers at Planet to interact effortlessly with our assets in space. We strive to minimize operational burden, and to automate all aspects of satellite operations. You will be a part of a team that will support, enable and engage with the end users to iterate on these systems. In this role, you will tackle and solve a broad array of engineering problems in a dynamic environment.

This is a full-time, remote position based in the United States or Canada. If located near an office, you are expected to work from that office 3 days per week.

Impact You'll Own:
• Maintain and augment the automation and services ultimately responsible for the operation of Planet’s satellites. This includes but is not limited to:
• Specify and implement new HTTP APIs and help improve existing ones.
• Evaluate and choose appropriate software technologies.
• Design and deploy critical infrastructure.
• Contribute with a team that values open and honest communication, collaboration, self-learning and initiative to solve problems big and small.
• Work closely with various teams across Planet, ranging from Satellite and Ground Operations, to Pipeline and Product teams to ensure the satellite constellation meets all of the needs to achieve Planet’s missions.

What You Bring:
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Aerospace Engineering, or similar.
• 4+ years of relevant work experience.
• Experience in Python programming.
• Experience in API design.
• Experience with automated testing, code review, and documentation.
• Experience with relational database technologies (e.g., Postgres, MySQL, etc.).
• Experience with maintaining peer-reviewed code and continuous integration and deployment workflows and tooling like GitLab CI and Docker or similar.
• Proficient Linux skills.
• Ability to be a self-starter for solving problems in a dynamic environment.
• Ability to clearly communicate and collaborate with cross-functional teams.

What Makes You Stand Out:
• Experience building services that leverage cloud-based infrastructure and tooling such as AWS or GCP.
• Experience maintaining infrastructure with Ansible or similar (eg. Kubernetes, Terraform).
• Experience working with monitoring tools like Datadog, Sentry, or equivalent.
• Experience working in microservice environments.
• Knowledge of aerospace concepts.

We are seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented Silicon Substrate Design Engineer to drive the physical design and layout implementation of next-generation 2D, 2.5D and 3D heterogeneous packaging architectures. Specializing in Silicon Interposer and Redistribution Layer (RDL) technologies, you will collaborate closely with ASIC architecture, foundry partners, OSATs, and Signal/Power Integrity (SI/PI) teams to deliver robust, high-density interconnect solutions for high-performance computing (HPC), AI accelerators, and high-speed networking applications.

Skills : IC Package Design & 2D, 2.5D & 3D packaging architectures is a must

Key Responsibilities

● Physical Layout & Routing: Own the end-to-end physical design, floor planning, and layout of passive/active silicon interposers and high-density RDL fan-out packages.1

● High-Density Interconnects: Execute complex routing for ultra-fine-pitch microbumps, C4 bumps, TSVs (Through-Silicon Vias), microvias, and pillar structures.1

● Interface Implementation: Route critical high-speed, high-bandwidth interfaces such as HBM, PCIe Gen 5/Gen 6, and die-to-die interfaces with stringent impedance controls.1

● Design Optimization: Actively drive die-package-board co-optimization (DPCO) by mapping bump assignments and floorplans across multiple disparate chiplets.1

● Constraint Implementation: Incorporate layout constraints provided by SI/PI teams, optimizing power delivery networks (PDN) and implementing shielding strategies to minimize crosstalk, insertion loss, and IR drop.1

● Verification: Run and resolve comprehensive physical verification checks, including Design Rule Checks (DRC), Layout Versus Schematic (LVS), connectivity, and antenna rules.1

● DFM & Yield: Collaborate with leading silicon foundries and OSATs to ensure strict alignment

with Design for Manufacturing (DFM), Design for Assembly (DFA), and reliability standards to maximize production yields.1

Required Skills & Qualifications Technical Expertise

● Deep understanding of 2.5D/3D advanced packaging architectures, wafer-level packaging (WLP), and substrate technologies.1

● Mastery of layout routing constraints, stack-up optimization, and silicon processing limits (e.g., fine-line/space lithography down to sub-micron scales for RDL).1

● Solid grasp of electromagnetic and transmission line fundamentals, signal integrity constraints, and thermal-mechanical stress factors in silicon-interposer assemblies.1 Tool & EDA Proficiency

● Extensive hands-on experience with advanced IC packaging and IC layout tools, including:

● Cadence Suite: APD, Allegro Package Designer Plus, Innovus, Virtuoso.1

● Verification: Siemens EDA Calibre, Ansys (HFSS/SIwave for layout review), and IC Validator.1

Soft Skills & Experience

● Excellent cross-functional communication skills, with a proven ability to bridge gaps between silicon design teams and manufacturing foundries.1

● Strong debugging and problem-solving skills when addressing complex DRC violations and multi-die routing bottlenecks.1

Education & Experience Requirements

● Education: Bachelor’s, Master’s, or Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering, Electronics, Microelectronics, Materials Science, or a closely related field.1

● Experience (Engineer): 3+ years of experience in silicon physical design or IC package layout.1

● Experience (Senior/Staff Engineer): 6–10+ years of hands-on experience delivering production-ready 2.5D/3D interposer or high-density fan-out (HDFO) designs from concept to tape-ou
